revision.comment: 松井塚古墳（大阪府太子町にあった古墳）。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 松井塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|280px]]
家形石棺形石槨
（太子町立太子・和みの広場に移築・展示） |別名 = |所属 = [[磯長谷古墳群]] |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]山田 |緯度度 = |緯度分 = |緯度秒 = |経度度 = |経度分 = |経度秒 = |ISO = JP-27 |形状 = （推定）[[方墳]] |規模 = |埋葬施設 = [[横穴式石室]]
（内部に家形石棺形石槨） |出土品 = 人骨・[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]中葉 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = 石棺（大阪府指定文化財） |特記事項 = 墳丘は非現存 |地図 = |アイコン = 方墳 |ラベル = 松井塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} '''松井塚古墳'''（まついづかこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]山田にあった[[古墳]]。[[磯長谷古墳群]]を構成した古墳の1つ。石棺は大阪府指定有形文化財に指定されている。 現在では墳丘は失われている。 == 概要 == 大阪府南東部、二上山山麓の磯長谷において、谷を形成する尾根の南斜面に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|松井塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。[[1958年]]（[[昭和]]33年）に民家の井戸掘削に伴い発見され、同年に大阪府教育委員会による発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|松井塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 墳形は明らかでないが、一説に方形と推定される{{Sfn|松井塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。主体部の埋葬施設は[[横穴式石室]]で、南方に開口し、石室の構築にあたっては[[唐尺]]の使用が推定される史跡説明板。。石室の床面には礫石が敷き詰められ、その上に[[凝灰岩]]（[[二上山]]産）製の刳抜式家形石棺形の石槨が据えられていた{{Sfn|松井塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。石槨は長さ2.5メートル・幅1.3メートルを測り{{Sfn|松井塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}、石槨前には石扉を伴う{{Sfn|松井塚古墳（平凡社）|1986}}。石槨内は盗掘に遭っていたが、調査では人骨（2体分か）や[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]]片のみが検出されている{{Sfn|松井塚古墳（平凡社）|1986}}。 この松井塚古墳は、[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]中葉頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|松井塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。発見当初は[[推古天皇]]の[[陪塚]]（陪冢）の可能性があるとして注目されたほか[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html 松井塚古墳の石槨]（太子町ホームページ）。、現在でも石室の規模が[[薄葬令|大化の薄葬令]]において王以上に該当する点で注目される{{Sfn|松井塚古墳（平凡社）|1986}}。また[[横口式石槨]]の変遷を考察するうえでも重要視される古墳である。 石棺は[[1973年]]（昭和48年）に大阪府指定有形文化財に指定され、現在では太子町立太子・和みの広場（[[叡福寺]]に隣接）で保存されている。 File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|石槨 横景 File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|石槨内部 == 文化財 == === 大阪府指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 松井塚古墳石棺（考古資料） - 1973年（昭和48年）3月30日指定[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html 大阪府内指定文化財一覧表]（大阪府ホームページ）の太子町ファイルより。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=松井塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 28 大阪府の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|松井塚古墳（平凡社）|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=玉井功|year=1989|chapter=松井塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|松井塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1958|chapter=|title=太子町松井塚古墳発掘調査概要|publisher=大阪府教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=松井塚古墳と出土土器|title=終末期古墳と横口式石槨|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[磯長谷古墳群]] * [[横口式石槨]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html 松井塚古墳の石槨] - 太子町ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:まついつかこふん}} [[Category:磯長谷古墳群]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:大阪府太子町の歴史]] [[Category:方墳]]

家形石棺形石槨
（太子町立太子・和みの広場に移築・展示） |別名 = |所属 = [[磯長谷古墳群]] |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]山田 |緯度度 = |緯度分 = |緯度秒 = |経度度 = |経度分 = |経度秒 = |ISO = JP-27 |形状 = （推定）[[方墳]] |規模 = |埋葬施設 = [[横穴式石室]]
（内部に家形石棺形石槨） |出土品 = 人骨・[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]中葉 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = 石棺（大阪府指定文化財） |特記事項 = 墳丘は非現存 |地図 = |アイコン = 方墳 |ラベル = 松井塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} '''松井塚古墳'''（まついづかこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]山田にあった[[古墳]]。[[磯長谷古墳群]]を構成した古墳の1つ。石棺は大阪府指定有形文化財に指定されている。 現在では墳丘は失われている。 == 概要 == 大阪府南東部、[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]山麓の磯長谷において、谷を形成する尾根の南斜面に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|松井塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。[[1958年]]（[[昭和]]33年）に民家の井戸掘削に伴い発見され、同年に大阪府教育委員会による発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|松井塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 墳形は明らかでないが、一説に方形と推定される{{Sfn|松井塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。主体部の埋葬施設は[[横穴式石室]]で、南方に開口し、石室の構築にあたっては[[唐尺]]の使用が推定される史跡説明板。。石室の床面には礫石が敷き詰められ、その上に[[凝灰岩]]（[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]産）製の刳抜式家形石棺形の石槨が据えられていた{{Sfn|松井塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。石槨は長さ2.5メートル・幅1.3メートルを測り{{Sfn|松井塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}、石槨前には石扉を伴う{{Sfn|松井塚古墳（平凡社）|1986}}。石槨内は盗掘に遭っていたが、調査では人骨（2体分か）や[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]]片のみが検出されている{{Sfn|松井塚古墳（平凡社）|1986}}。 この松井塚古墳は、[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]中葉頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|松井塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。発見当初は[[推古天皇]]の[[陪塚]]（陪冢）の可能性があるとして注目されたほか[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html 松井塚古墳の石槨]（太子町ホームページ）。、現在でも石室の規模が[[薄葬令|大化の薄葬令]]において王以上に該当する点で注目される{{Sfn|松井塚古墳（平凡社）|1986}}。また[[横口式石槨]]の変遷を考察するうえでも重要視される古墳である。 石棺は[[1973年]]（昭和48年）に大阪府指定有形文化財に指定され、現在では太子町立太子・和みの広場（[[叡福寺]]に隣接）に移築・展示されている。 File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|石槨 横景 File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|石槨内部 == 文化財 == === 大阪府指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 松井塚古墳石棺（考古資料） - 1973年（昭和48年）3月30日指定[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html 大阪府内指定文化財一覧表]（大阪府ホームページ）の太子町ファイルより。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=松井塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 28 大阪府の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|松井塚古墳（平凡社）|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=玉井功|year=1989|chapter=松井塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|松井塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1958|chapter=|title=太子町松井塚古墳発掘調査概要|publisher=大阪府教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=山本彰|year=2007|chapter=松井塚古墳と出土土器|title=終末期古墳と横口式石槨|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[磯長谷古墳群]] * [[横口式石槨]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html 松井塚古墳の石槨] - 太子町ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:まついつかこふん}} [[Category:磯長谷古墳群]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:大阪府太子町の歴史]] [[Category:方墳]]

家形石棺形石槨（大阪府指定文化財）
（太子町立太子・和みの広場に移築・展示） |別名 = |所属 = [[磯長谷古墳群]] |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]山田 | 緯度度 = 34| 緯度分 = 31| 緯度秒 = 08.7 | 経度度 = 135| 経度分 = 38| 経度秒 = 25.2 |ISO = JP-27 |形状 = （推定）[[方墳]] |規模 = |埋葬施設 = [[横穴式石室]]
（内部に家形石棺形石槨） |出土品 = 人骨・[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]中葉 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = 石棺（大阪府指定文化財） |特記事項 = 墳丘は非現存 |地図 = |アイコン = 方墳 |ラベル = 松井塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} '''松井塚古墳'''（まついづかこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]山田にあった[[古墳]]。[[磯長谷古墳群]]を構成した古墳の1つ。石棺は大阪府指定有形文化財に指定されている。 現在では墳丘は失われている。 == 概要 == 大阪府南東部、[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]山麓の磯長谷において、谷を形成する尾根の南斜面に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|松井塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。[[1958年]]（[[昭和]]33年）に民家の井戸掘削に伴い発見され、同年に大阪府教育委員会による発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|松井塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 墳形は明らかでないが、一説に方形と推定される{{Sfn|松井塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。埋葬施設は[[横穴式石室]]で、南方に開口し、石室の構築にあたっては[[唐尺]]の使用が推定される史跡説明板。。石室の床面には礫石が敷き詰められ、その上に[[凝灰岩]]（[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]産）製の刳抜式家形石棺形の石槨が据えられていた{{Sfn|松井塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。石槨は長さ2.5メートル・幅1.3メートルを測り{{Sfn|松井塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}、石槨前には石扉を伴う{{Sfn|松井塚古墳（平凡社）|1986}}。石槨内は盗掘に遭っていたが、調査では人骨（2体分か）や[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]]片のみが検出されている{{Sfn|松井塚古墳（平凡社）|1986}}。 この松井塚古墳は、[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]中葉頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|松井塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。発見当初は[[推古天皇]]の[[陪塚]]（陪冢）の可能性があるとして注目されたほか[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html 松井塚古墳の石槨]（太子町ホームページ）。、現在でも石室の規模が[[薄葬令|大化の薄葬令]]において王以上に該当する点で注目される{{Sfn|松井塚古墳（平凡社）|1986}}。また[[横口式石槨]]の変遷を考察するうえでも重要視される古墳である。 石棺は[[1973年]]（昭和48年）に大阪府指定有形文化財に指定され、現在では太子町立太子・和みの広場（[[叡福寺]]に隣接）に移築・展示されている。 File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|石槨 横景 File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|石槨内部 == 文化財 == === 大阪府指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 松井塚古墳石棺（考古資料） - 1973年（昭和48年）3月30日指定[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html 大阪府内指定文化財一覧表]（大阪府ホームページ）の太子町ファイルより。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=松井塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 28 大阪府の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|松井塚古墳（平凡社）|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=玉井功|year=1989|chapter=松井塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|松井塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1958|chapter=|title=太子町松井塚古墳発掘調査概要|publisher=大阪府教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=山本彰|year=2007|chapter=松井塚古墳と出土土器|title=終末期古墳と横口式石槨|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[磯長谷古墳群]] * [[横口式石槨]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html 松井塚古墳の石槨] - revision.text:
{{Japanese Kofun Infobox | name = Matsuitsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|280px]]
House-shaped stone coffin (Osaka Prefectural Cultural Property)
(Relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi Plaza, managed by Taishi Town) | alias = | group = [[Isonokami Kofun Cluster]] | location = [[Yamada, Taishi Town]], [[Minamikawachi District]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | latitude = 34.5189 | longitude = 135.6403 | type = Presumed [[Square Kofun]] | size = | burial_chamber = [[Horizontal stone chamber]]
(Interior contains house-shaped stone coffin) | artifacts = Human remains, [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]] | burial_mound = Not preserved | period = Mid [[7th century]] | occupant = Unknown | historic_site = None | cultural_property = Stone coffin (Osaka Prefectural Designation) | notable = The burial mound no longer exists }} '''Matsuitsuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) formerly located in [[Yamada, Taishi Town]], [[Minamikawachi District]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It was part of the [[Isonokami Kofun Cluster]], and its stone coffin has been designated as an Osaka Prefectural Cultural Property. The mound itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuitsuka Kofun was built on the southern slope of a valley ridge in the [[Isonokami Kofun Cluster]], located at the foothills of [[Mount Nijo]] in southeastern Osaka. The tomb was discovered in [[1958]] (Showa 33) during the digging of a well at a private residence. It was excavated by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education later that year{{Sfn|Matsuitsuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Although the mound's shape is not confirmed, it is presumed to have been square{{Sfn|Matsuitsuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial chamber is a [[horizontal stone chamber]] that opens to the south, and its construction is thought to have involved the use of the [[Tang measurement system]]Information plaque at the site.. The chamber floor was lined with gravel, on top of which a hollowed-out house-shaped stone coffin made from [[tuff]] quarried from [[Mount Nijo]] was placed{{Sfn|Matsuitsuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The stone coffin measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Sfn|Matsuitsuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it features a stone door{{Sfn|Matsuitsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. Inside, only fragments of human remains (possibly two individuals) and artifacts such as [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]] were recovered due to prior looting{{Sfn|Matsuitsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The Matsuitsuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-[[7th century]] during the [[kofun period|late kofun period]]{{Sfn|Matsuitsuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, it was considered a potential [[accompanying mound]] of [[Empress Suiko]]'s tomb[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuitsuka Kofun Coffin] (Taishi Town website).. The scale of the chamber corresponds to criteria for royals as defined by the [[Taika no Reform]]'s [[law of simplified burials]], making it a noteworthy site{{Sfn|Matsuitsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. Additionally, the kofun holds significance in the study of transitions in [[horizontal stone chambers]]. In [[1973]] (Showa 48), the stone coffin was designated as an Osaka Prefectural Cultural Property. It is now relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi Plaza near [[Eifukuji Temple]]. Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Stone coffin (side view) Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Stone coffin (interior) 松井塚古墳出土 土師器.JPG|Haji ware found within the coffin
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the [[Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] in Osaka.}} == Cultural Property == === Osaka Prefectural Designations === * Cultural Property ** Matsuitsuka Kofun Stone Coffin (Archaeological artifact) - Designated on March 30, 1973[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties List] (from Taishi Town section of Osaka Prefecture website).. == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article writing)}} * Information plaque * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Matsuitsuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 28 Osaka-fu no Chimei|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Matsuitsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Tamai Isao|year=1989|chapter=Matsuitsuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Matsuitsuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for article writing)}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=1958|chapter=|title=Taishi Town Matsuitsuka Kofun Excavation Report|publisher=Osaka Prefectural Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Yamamoto Akira|year=2007|chapter=Matsuitsuka Kofun and Excavated Ceramics|title=End Kofun Period Tombs and Horizontal Stone Chambers|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kobunkan]]|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * [[Isonokami Kofun Cluster]] * [[Horizontal stone chamber]] == External Links == * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuitsuka Kofun Coffin] - Taishi Town Website {{DEFAULTSORT:Matsuitsuka Kofun}} [[Category:Isonokami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi Town, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]]

(Interior contains house-shaped stone coffin) | artifacts = Human remains, [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]] | burial_mound = Not preserved | period = Mid [[7th century]] | occupant = Unknown | historic_site = None | cultural_property = Stone coffin (Osaka Prefectural Designation) | notable = The burial mound no longer exists }} '''Matsuitsuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) formerly located in [[Yamada, Taishi Town]], [[Minamikawachi District]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. Inside, only fragments of human remains (possibly two individuals) and artifacts such as [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]] were recovered due to prior looting{{Sfn|Matsuitsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The Matsuitsuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-[[7th century]] during the [[kofun period|late kofun period]]{{Sfn|Matsuitsuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, it was considered a potential [[accompanying mound]] of [[Empress Suiko]]'s tomb[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuitsuka Kofun Coffin] (Taishi Town website).. The scale of the chamber corresponds to criteria for royals as defined by the [[Taika no Reform]]'s [[law of simplified burials]], making it a noteworthy site{{Sfn|Matsuitsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. Additionally, the kofun holds significance in the study of transitions in [[horizontal stone chambers]]. In [[1973]] (Showa 48), the stone coffin was designated as an Osaka Prefectural Cultural Property. It is now relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi Plaza near [[Eifukuji Temple]]. {{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the [[Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] in Osaka.}} == Cultural Property == === Osaka Prefectural Designations === * Cultural Property ** Matsuitsuka Kofun Stone Coffin (Archaeological artifact) - Designated on March 30, 1973[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties List] (from Taishi Town section of Osaka Prefecture website).. == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article writing)}} * Information plaque * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Matsuitsuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 28 Osaka-fu no Chimei|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Matsuitsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Tamai Isao|year=1989|chapter=Matsuitsuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Matsuitsuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for article writing)}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=1958|chapter=|title=Taishi Town Matsuitsuka Kofun Excavation Report|publisher=Osaka Prefectural Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Yamamoto Akira|year=2007|chapter=Matsuitsuka Kofun and Excavated Ceramics|title=End Kofun Period Tombs and Horizontal Stone Chambers|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kobunkan]]|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * [[Isonokami Kofun Cluster]] * [[Horizontal stone chamber]] == External Links == * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuitsuka Kofun Coffin] - Taishi Town Website {{DEFAULTSORT:Matsuitsuka Kofun}} [[Category:Isonokami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi Town, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Kk]][[ja:松井塚古墳]]

{{Japanese Kofun Infobox | name = Matsuitsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|280px]]
House-shaped stone coffin (Osaka Prefectural Cultural Property)
(Relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi Plaza, managed by Taishi Town) | alias = | group = [[Isonokami Kofun Cluster]] | location = [[Yamada, Taishi Town]], [[Minamikawachi District]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | latitude = 34.5189 | longitude = 135.6403 | type = Presumed [[Square Kofun]] | size = | burial_chamber = [[Horizontal stone chamber]]
(Interior contains house-shaped stone coffin) | artifacts = Human remains, [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]] | burial_mound = Not preserved | period = Mid [[7th century]] | occupant = Unknown | historic_site = None | cultural_property = Stone coffin (Osaka Prefectural Designation) | notable = The burial mound no longer exists }} '''Matsuitsuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) formerly located in [[Yamada, Taishi Town]], [[Minamikawachi District]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It was part of the [[Isonokami Kofun Cluster]], and its stone coffin has been designated as an Osaka Prefectural Cultural Property. The mound itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuitsuka Kofun was built on the southern slope of a valley ridge in the [[Isonokami Kofun Cluster]], located at the foothills of [[Mount Nijo]] in southeastern Osaka. The tomb was discovered in [[1958]] (Showa 33) during the digging of a well at a private residence. It was excavated by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education later that year{{Sfn|Matsuitsuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Although the mound's shape is not confirmed, it is presumed to have been square{{Sfn|Matsuitsuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial chamber is a [[horizontal stone chamber]] that opens to the south, and its construction is thought to have involved the use of the [[Tang measurement system]]Information plaque at the site.. The chamber floor was lined with gravel, on top of which a hollowed-out house-shaped stone coffin made from [[tuff]] quarried from [[Mount Nijo]] was placed{{Sfn|Matsuitsuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The stone coffin measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Sfn|Matsuitsuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it features a stone door{{Sfn|Matsuitsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. Inside, only fragments of human remains (possibly two individuals) and artifacts such as [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]] were recovered due to prior looting{{Sfn|Matsuitsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The Matsuitsuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-[[7th century]] during the [[kofun period|late kofun period]]{{Sfn|Matsuitsuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, it was considered a potential [[accompanying mound]] of [[Empress Suiko]]'s tomb[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuitsuka Kofun Coffin] (Taishi Town website).. The scale of the chamber corresponds to criteria for royals as defined by the [[Taika no Reform]]'s [[law of simplified burials]], making it a noteworthy site{{Sfn|Matsuitsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. Additionally, the kofun holds significance in the study of transitions in [[horizontal stone chambers]]. In [[1973]] (Showa 48), the stone coffin was designated as an Osaka Prefectural Cultural Property. It is now relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi Plaza near [[Eifukuji Temple]]. Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Stone coffin (side view) Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Stone coffin (interior) 松井塚古墳出土 土師器.JPG|Haji ware found within the coffin

{{Japanese Kofun Infobox | name = Matsuitsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|280px]]
House-shaped stone coffin (Osaka Prefectural Cultural Property)
(Relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi Plaza, managed by Taishi Town) | alias = | group = [[Isonokami Kofun Cluster]] | location = [[Yamada, Taishi Town]], [[Minamikawachi District]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | latitude = 34.5189 | longitude = 135.6403 | type = Presumed [[Square Kofun]] | size = | burial_chamber = [[Horizontal stone chamber]]
(Interior contains house-shaped stone coffin) | artifacts = Human remains, [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]] | burial_mound = Not preserved | period = Mid [[7th century]] | occupant = Unknown | historic_site = None | cultural_property = Stone coffin (Osaka Prefectural Designation) | notable = The burial mound no longer exists }} '''Matsuitsuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) formerly located in [[Yamada, Taishi Town]], [[Minamikawachi District]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It was part of the [[Isonokami Kofun Cluster]], and its stone coffin has been designated as an Osaka Prefectural Cultural Property. The mound itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuitsuka Kofun was built on the southern slope of a valley ridge in the [[Isonokami Kofun Cluster]], located at the foothills of [[Mount Nijo]] in southeastern Osaka. The tomb was discovered in [[1958]] (Showa 33) during the digging of a well at a private residence. It was excavated by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education later that year{{Sfn|Matsuitsuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Although the mound's shape is not confirmed, it is presumed to have been square{{Sfn|Matsuitsuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial chamber is a [[horizontal stone chamber]] that opens to the south, and its construction is thought to have involved the use of the [[Tang measurement system]]Information plaque at the site.. The chamber floor was lined with gravel, on top of which a hollowed-out house-shaped stone coffin made from [[tuff]] quarried from [[Mount Nijo]] was placed{{Sfn|Matsuitsuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The stone coffin measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Sfn|Matsuitsuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it features a stone door{{Sfn|Matsuitsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. Inside, only fragments of human remains (possibly two individuals) and artifacts such as [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]] were recovered due to prior looting{{Sfn|Matsuitsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The Matsuitsuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-[[7th century]] during the [[kofun period|late kofun period]]{{Sfn|Matsuitsuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, it was considered a potential [[accompanying mound]] of [[Empress Suiko]]'s tomb[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuitsuka Kofun Coffin] (Taishi Town website).. The scale of the chamber corresponds to criteria for royals as defined by the [[Taika no Reform]]'s [[law of simplified burials]], making it a noteworthy site{{Sfn|Matsuitsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. Additionally, the kofun holds significance in the study of transitions in [[horizontal stone chambers]]. In [[1973]] (Showa 48), the stone coffin was designated as an Osaka Prefectural Cultural Property. It is now relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi Plaza near [[Eifukuji Temple]]. {{Japanese Kofun Infobox | name = Matsuitsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|280px]]
House-shaped stone coffin (Osaka Prefectural Cultural Property)
(Relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi Plaza, managed by Taishi Town) | alias = | group = [[Isonokami Kofun Cluster]] | location = [[Yamada, Taishi Town]], [[Minamikawachi District]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | latitude = 34.5189 | longitude = 135.6403 | type = Presumed [[Square Kofun]] | size = | burial_chamber = [[Horizontal stone chamber]]
(Interior contains house-shaped stone coffin) | artifacts = Human remains, [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]] | burial_mound = Not preserved | period = Mid [[7th century]] | occupant = Unknown | historic_site = None | cultural_property = Stone coffin (Osaka Prefectural Designation) | notable = The burial mound no longer exists }} '''Matsuitsuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) formerly located in [[Yamada, Taishi Town]], [[Minamikawachi District]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It was part of the [[Isonokami Kofun Cluster]], and its stone coffin has been designated as an Osaka Prefectural Cultural Property. The mound itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuitsuka Kofun was built on the southern slope of a valley ridge in the [[Isonokami Kofun Cluster]], located at the foothills of [[Mount Nijo]] in southeastern Osaka. The tomb was discovered in [[1958]] (Showa 33) during the digging of a well at a private residence. It was excavated by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education later that year{{Sfn|Matsuitsuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Although the mound's shape is not confirmed, it is presumed to have been square{{Sfn|Matsuitsuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial chamber is a [[horizontal stone chamber]] that opens to the south, and its construction is thought to have involved the use of the [[Tang measurement system]]Information plaque at the site.. The chamber floor was lined with gravel, on top of which a hollowed-out house-shaped stone coffin made from [[tuff]] quarried from [[Mount Nijo]] was placed{{Sfn|Matsuitsuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The stone coffin measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Sfn|Matsuitsuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it features a stone door{{Sfn|Matsuitsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. Inside, only fragments of human remains (possibly two individuals) and artifacts such as [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]] were recovered due to prior looting{{Sfn|Matsuitsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The Matsuitsuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-[[7th century]] during the [[kofun period|late kofun period]]{{Sfn|Matsuitsuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, it was considered a potential [[Baizuka|accompanying mound]] of [[Empress Suiko]]'s tomb[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuitsuka Kofun Coffin] (Taishi Town website).. The scale of the chamber corresponds to criteria for royals as defined by the [[Taika no Reform]]'s [[law of simplified burials]], making it a noteworthy site{{Sfn|Matsuitsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. Additionally, the kofun holds significance in the study of transitions in [[horizontal stone chambers]]. In [[1973]] (Showa 48), the stone coffin was designated as an Osaka Prefectural Cultural Property. It is now relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi Plaza near [[Eifukuji Temple]]. Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Stone coffin (side view) Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Stone coffin (interior) 松井塚古墳出土 土師器.JPG|Haji ware found within the coffin
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the [[Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] in Osaka.}} == Cultural Property == === Osaka Prefectural Designations === * Cultural Property ''' Matsuitsuka Kofun Stone Coffin (Archaeological artifact) - {{Japanese kofun |name = Matsuizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|280px]]
House-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku (Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property)
(Relocated and exhibited at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba, Taishi Town) |alias = |affiliation = {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |latitude_deg = 34|latitude_min = 31|latitude_sec = 08.7 |longitude_deg = 135|longitude_min = 38|longitude_sec = 25.2 |ISO = JP-27 |type = (Presumed) {{ill|Square burial mound|ja|方墳}} |size = |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}
(With house-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku inside) |grave_goods = Human remains, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |satellite = |construction_period = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_designation = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Stone coffin (Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_icon = Square burial mound |label = Matsuizuka Kofun |label_position = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It was part of the {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. The stone coffin from this kofun has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The burial mound itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|ja|昭和33年}} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s shape is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang尺|ja|唐尺}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新}}, as its scale corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Yokoguchi sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}. The stone coffin was designated as an Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|ja|叡福寺}}. (With house-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku inside) |grave_goods = Human remains, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |satellite = |construction_period = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_designation = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Stone coffin (Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_icon = Square burial mound |label = Matsuizuka Kofun |label_position = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It was part of the {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. The stone coffin from this kofun has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The burial mound itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|ja|昭和33年}} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s shape is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang尺|ja|唐尺}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新}}, as its scale corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Yokoguchi sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}. The stone coffin was designated as an Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|ja|叡福寺}}. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s shape is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新}}, as its scale corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Yokoguchi sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}. The stone coffin was designated as an Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|ja|叡福寺}}. File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Sekkaku (Side view) File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Sekkaku interior File:松井塚古墳出土_土師器.JPG|Haji ware from the sekkaku interior
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|lt=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Matsuizuka Kofun Stone Coffin (Archaeological Material) - Designated on March 30, 1973[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (from Taishi Town section).. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * On-site explanation board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系]] 28: Osaka Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Isao Tamai|year=1989|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|Not directly cited in the article}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1958|chapter=|title=Summary of Excavation of Matsuizuka Kofun, Taishi Town|publisher=Osaka Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Akira Yamamoto|year=2007|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun and Unearthed Pottery|title=Kofun of the Late Period and Yokoguchi Sekkaku|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} * {{ill|Yokoguchi Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] - The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新}}, as its scale corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Yokoguchi sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}. The stone coffin was designated as an Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|ja|叡福寺}}. {{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|lt=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Matsuizuka Kofun Stone Coffin (Archaeological Material) - Designated on March 30, 1973[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (from Taishi Town section).. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * On-site explanation board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系]] 28: Osaka Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Isao Tamai|year=1989|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|Not directly cited in the article}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1958|chapter=|title=Summary of Excavation of Matsuizuka Kofun, Taishi Town|publisher=Osaka Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Akira Yamamoto|year=2007|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun and Unearthed Pottery|title=Kofun of the Late Period and Yokoguchi Sekkaku|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} * {{ill|Yokoguchi Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] - {{Infobox ancient site |name = Matsuizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|280px]]
House-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku (Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property)
(Relocated and exhibited at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba, Taishi Town) |alias = |affiliation = {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |latitude_deg = 34|latitude_min = 31|latitude_sec = 08.7 |longitude_deg = 135|longitude_min = 38|longitude_sec = 25.2 |ISO = JP-27 |type = (Presumed) {{ill|Square burial mound|ja|方墳}} |size = |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}
(With house-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku inside) |grave_goods = Human remains, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |satellite = |construction_period = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_designation = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Stone coffin (Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_icon = Square burial mound |label = Matsuizuka Kofun |label_position = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It was part of the {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. The stone coffin from this kofun has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The burial mound itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|ja|昭和33年}} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s shape is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新}}, as its scale corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Yokoguchi sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}. The stone coffin was designated as an Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|ja|叡福寺}}. File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Sekkaku (Side view) File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Sekkaku interior File:松井塚古墳出土_土師器.JPG|Haji ware from the sekkaku interior
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|lt=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Matsuizuka Kofun Stone Coffin (Archaeological Material) - Designated on March 30, 1973[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (from Taishi Town section).. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * On-site explanation board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系]] 28: Osaka Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Isao Tamai|year=1989|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|Not directly cited in the article}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1958|chapter=|title=Summary of Excavation of Matsuizuka Kofun, Taishi Town|publisher=Osaka Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Akira Yamamoto|year=2007|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun and Unearthed Pottery|title=Kofun of the Late Period and Yokoguchi Sekkaku|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} * {{ill|Yokoguchi Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] - Taishi Town Website [[Category:Isonokami Burial Mounds]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Taishi, Osaka|History]] [[Category:Square burial mounds]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Matsuitsuka Kofun}} [[Category:Isonokami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Taishi Town, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Matsuizuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Matsuizuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Matsuizuka Kofun]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Matsuizuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Matsuizuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Matsuizuka Kofun]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Matsuizuka Kofun]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Matsuizuka Kofun]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Isonokami Valley Tumuli]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[ja:松井塚古墳]]

Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新}}, as its scale corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Yokoguchi sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}. The stone coffin was designated as an Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|ja|叡福寺}}. {{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|lt=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Matsuizuka Kofun Stone Coffin (Archaeological Material) - {{Infobox ancient site |name = Matsuizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|280px]]
House-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku (Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property)
(Relocated and exhibited at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba, Taishi Town) |alias = |affiliation = {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |latitude_deg = 34|latitude_min = 31|latitude_sec = 08.7 |longitude_deg = 135|longitude_min = 38|longitude_sec = 25.2 |ISO = JP-27 |type = (Presumed) {{ill|Square burial mound|ja|方墳}} |size = |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}
(With house-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku inside) |grave_goods = Human remains, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |satellite = |construction_period = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_designation = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Stone coffin (Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_icon = Square burial mound |label = Matsuizuka Kofun |label_position = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It was part of the {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. The stone coffin from this kofun has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The burial mound itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|ja|昭和33年}} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s shape is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新}}, as its scale corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Yokoguchi sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}. The stone coffin was designated as an Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|ja|叡福寺}}. {{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|lt=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Matsuizuka Kofun Stone Coffin (Archaeological Material) - Designated on March 30, 1973[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (from Taishi Town section).. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * On-site explanation board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系]] 28: Osaka Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Isao Tamai|year=1989|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|Not directly cited in the article}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1958|chapter=|title=Summary of Excavation of Matsuizuka Kofun, Taishi Town|publisher=Osaka Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Akira Yamamoto|year=2007|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun and Unearthed Pottery|title=Kofun of the Late Period and Yokoguchi Sekkaku|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} * {{ill|Yokoguchi Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] - revision.comment: Bumping for adding the Kofun template
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Matsuizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|280px]]
House-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku (Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property)
(Relocated and exhibited at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba, Taishi Town) |alias = |affiliation = {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |latitude_deg = 34|latitude_min = 31|latitude_sec = 08.7 |longitude_deg = 135|longitude_min = 38|longitude_sec = 25.2 |ISO = JP-27 |type = (Presumed) {{ill|Square burial mound|ja|方墳}} |size = |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}
(With house-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku inside) |grave_goods = Human remains, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |satellite = |construction_period = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_designation = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Stone coffin (Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_icon = Square burial mound |label = Matsuizuka Kofun |label_position = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It was part of the {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. The stone coffin from this kofun has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The burial mound itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|ja|昭和33年}} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s shape is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新}}, as its scale corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Yokoguchi sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}. The stone coffin was designated as an Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|ja|叡福寺}}. File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Sekkaku (Side view) File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Sekkaku interior File:松井塚古墳出土_土師器.JPG|Haji ware from the sekkaku interior
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|lt=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Matsuizuka Kofun Stone Coffin (Archaeological Material) - Designated on March 30, 1973[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (from Taishi Town section).. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Akira Yamamoto|year=2007|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun and Unearthed Pottery|title=Kofun of the Late Period and Yokoguchi Sekkaku|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} * {{ill|Yokoguchi Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} == External Links == * (With house-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku inside) |grave_goods = Human remains, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |satellite = |construction_period = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_designation = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Stone coffin (Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_icon = Square burial mound |label = Matsuizuka Kofun |label_position = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It was part of the {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. The stone coffin from this kofun has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The burial mound itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|ja|昭和33年}} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s shape is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新}}, as its scale corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Yokoguchi sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}. The stone coffin was designated as an Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|ja|叡福寺}}. {{Infobox ancient site |name = Matsuizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|280px]]
House-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku (Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property)
(Relocated and exhibited at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba, Taishi Town) |alias = |affiliation = {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |latitude_deg = 34|latitude_min = 31|latitude_sec = 08.7 |longitude_deg = 135|longitude_min = 38|longitude_sec = 25.2 |ISO = JP-27 |type = (Presumed) {{ill|Square burial mound|ja|方墳}} |size = |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}
(With house-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku inside) |grave_goods = Human remains, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |satellite = |construction_period = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_designation = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Stone coffin (Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_icon = Square burial mound |label = Matsuizuka Kofun |label_position = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It was part of the {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. The stone coffin from this kofun has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The burial mound itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|ja|昭和33年}} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s shape is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新}}, as its scale corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Yokoguchi sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}. The stone coffin was designated as an Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|ja|叡福寺}}. File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Sekkaku (Side view) File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Sekkaku interior File:松井塚古墳出土_土師器.JPG|Haji ware from the sekkaku interior
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|lt=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture Designated (Relocated and exhibited at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba, Taishi Town) |alias = |affiliation = {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |latitude_deg = 34|latitude_min = 31|latitude_sec = 08.7 |longitude_deg = 135|longitude_min = 38|longitude_sec = 25.2 |ISO = JP-27 |type = (Presumed) {{ill|Square burial mound|ja|方墳}} |size = |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}
(With house-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku inside) |grave_goods = Human remains, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |satellite = |construction_period = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_designation = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Stone coffin (Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_icon = Square burial mound |label = Matsuizuka Kofun |label_position = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It was part of the {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. The stone coffin from this kofun has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The burial mound itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|ja|昭和33年}} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s shape is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新}}, as its scale corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Yokoguchi sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}. The stone coffin was designated as an Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|ja|叡福寺}}. File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Sekkaku (Side view) File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Sekkaku interior File:松井塚古墳出土_土師器.JPG|Haji ware from the sekkaku interior
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|lt=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Matsuizuka Kofun Stone Coffin (Archaeological Material) - Designated on March 30, 1973[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (from Taishi Town section).. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} Tamai|year=1989|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Akira Yamamoto|year=2007|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun and Unearthed Pottery|title=Kofun of the Late Period and Yokoguchi Sekkaku|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} * {{ill|Yokoguchi Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} == External Links == * revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Matsuizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|280px]]
House-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku (Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property)
(Relocated and exhibited at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba, Taishi Town) |alias = |affiliation = {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |latitude_deg = 34|latitude_min = 31|latitude_sec = 08.7 |longitude_deg = 135|longitude_min = 38|longitude_sec = 25.2 |ISO = JP-27 |type = (Presumed) {{ill|Square burial mound|ja|方墳}} |size = |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}
(With house-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku inside) |grave_goods = Human remains, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |satellite = |construction_period = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_designation = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Stone coffin (Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_icon = Square burial mound |label = Matsuizuka Kofun |label_position = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It was part of the {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. The stone coffin from this kofun has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The burial mound itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|ja|昭和33年}} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s shape is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新}}, as its scale corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Yokoguchi sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}. The stone coffin was designated as an Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|ja|叡福寺}}. File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Sekkaku (Side view) File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Sekkaku interior File:松井塚古墳出土_土師器.JPG|Haji ware from the sekkaku interior
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|lt=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Matsuizuka Kofun Stone Coffin (Archaeological Material) - Designated on March 30, 1973[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (from Taishi Town section).. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * On-site explanation board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系]] 28: Osaka Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Isao Tamai|year=1989|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|Not directly cited in the article}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1958|chapter=|title=Summary of Excavation of Matsuizuka Kofun, Taishi Town|publisher=Osaka Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Akira Yamamoto|year=2007|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun and Unearthed Pottery|title=Kofun of the Late Period and Yokoguchi Sekkaku|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} * {{ill|Yokoguchi Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} == External Links == *
