{{Infobox ancient site |name = Matsuizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|280px]]
House-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku (Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property)
(Relocated and exhibited at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba, Taishi Town) |alias = |affiliation = {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |latitude_deg = 34|latitude_min = 31|latitude_sec = 08.7 |longitude_deg = 135|longitude_min = 38|longitude_sec = 25.2 |ISO = JP-27 |type = (Presumed) {{ill|Square burial mound|ja|方墳}} |size = |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}
(With house-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku inside) |grave_goods = Human remains, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |satellite = |construction_period = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_designation = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Stone coffin (Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_icon = Square burial mound |label = Matsuizuka Kofun |label_position = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It was part of the {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. The stone coffin from this kofun has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The burial mound itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|ja|昭和33年}} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s shape is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新}}, as its scale corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Yokoguchi sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}. The stone coffin was designated as an Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|ja|叡福寺}}. File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Sekkaku (Side view) File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Sekkaku interior File:松井塚古墳出土_土師器.JPG|Haji ware from the sekkaku interior
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|lt=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Matsuizuka Kofun Stone Coffin (Archaeological Material) - Designated on March 30, 1973[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (from Taishi Town section).. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} It was part of the {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. The stone coffin from this kofun has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The burial mound itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|ja|昭和33年}} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新}}, as its scale corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Yokoguchi sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}. The stone coffin was designated as an Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|ja|叡福寺}}. (With house-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku inside) |grave_goods = Human remains, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |satellite = |construction_period = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_designation = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Stone coffin (Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_icon = Square burial mound |label = Matsuizuka Kofun |label_position = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It was part of the {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. The stone coffin from this kofun has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The burial mound itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|ja|昭和33年}} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s shape is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新}}, as its scale corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Yokoguchi sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}. The stone coffin was designated as an Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|ja|叡福寺}}. Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Matsuizuka Kofun Stone Coffin (Archaeological Material) - Designated on March 30, The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新}}, as its scale corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Yokoguchi sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}. The stone coffin was designated as an Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|ja|叡福寺}}. {{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|lt=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Matsuizuka Kofun Stone Coffin (Archaeological Material) - Designated on March 30, 1973[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (from Taishi Town section).. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Matsuizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|280px]]
House-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku (Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property)
(Relocated and exhibited at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba, Taishi Town) |alias = |affiliation = {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |latitude_deg = 34|latitude_min = 31|latitude_sec = 08.7 |longitude_deg = 135|longitude_min = 38|longitude_sec = 25.2 |ISO = JP-27 |type = (Presumed) {{ill|Square burial mound|ja|方墳}} |size = |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}
(With house-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku inside) |grave_goods = Human remains, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |satellite = |construction_period = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_designation = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Stone coffin (Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_icon = Square burial mound |label = Matsuizuka Kofun |label_position = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It was part of the {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. The stone coffin from this kofun has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The burial mound itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|ja|昭和33年}} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s shape is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新}}, as its scale corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Yokoguchi sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}. The stone coffin was designated as an Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|ja|叡福寺}}. {{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|lt=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Matsuizuka Kofun Stone Coffin (Archaeological Material) - Designated on March 30, 1973[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (from Taishi Town section).. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} revision.comment: Adding [[Category:横穴式石室]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:横穴式石室]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Matsuizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|280px]]
House-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku (Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property)
(Relocated and exhibited at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba, Taishi Town) |alias = |affiliation = {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |latitude_deg = 34|latitude_min = 31|latitude_sec = 08.7 |longitude_deg = 135|longitude_min = 38|longitude_sec = 25.2 |ISO = JP-27 |type = (Presumed) {{ill|Square burial mound|ja|方墳}} |size = |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}
(With house-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku inside) |grave_goods = Human remains, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |satellite = |construction_period = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_designation = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Stone coffin (Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_icon = Square burial mound |label = Matsuizuka Kofun |label_position = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It was part of the {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. The stone coffin from this kofun has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The burial mound itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|ja|昭和33年}} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s shape is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新}}, as its scale corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Yokoguchi sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}. The stone coffin was designated as an Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|ja|叡福寺}}. File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Sekkaku (Side view) File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Sekkaku interior File:松井塚古墳出土_土師器.JPG|Haji ware from the sekkaku interior
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|lt=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Matsuizuka Kofun Stone Coffin (Archaeological Material) - Designated on March 30, 1973[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (from Taishi Town section).. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * On-site explanation board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系]] 28: Osaka Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Isao Tamai|year=1989|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|Not directly cited in the article}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1958|chapter=|title=Summary of Excavation of Matsuizuka Kofun, Taishi Town|publisher=Osaka Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Akira Yamamoto|year=2007|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun and Unearthed Pottery|title=Kofun of the Late Period and Yokoguchi Sekkaku|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} * {{ill|Yokoguchi Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} == External Links == * revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Matsuizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|280px]]
House-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku (Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property)
(Relocated and exhibited at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba, Taishi Town) |alias = |affiliation = {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |latitude_deg = 34|latitude_min = 31|latitude_sec = 08.7 |longitude_deg = 135|longitude_min = 38|longitude_sec = 25.2 |ISO = JP-27 |type = (Presumed) {{ill|Square burial mound|ja|方墳}} |size = |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}
(With house-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku inside) |grave_goods = Human remains, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |satellite = |construction_period = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_designation = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Stone coffin (Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_icon = Square burial mound |label = Matsuizuka Kofun |label_position = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It was part of the {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. The stone coffin from this kofun has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The burial mound itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|ja|昭和33年}} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s shape is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新}}, as its scale corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Yokoguchi sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}. The stone coffin was designated as an Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|ja|叡福寺}}. File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Sekkaku (Side view) File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Sekkaku interior File:松井塚古墳出土_土師器.JPG|Haji ware from the sekkaku interior
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|lt=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Matsuizuka Kofun Stone Coffin (Archaeological Material) - Designated on March 30, 1973[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (from Taishi Town section).. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * On-site explanation board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系]] 28: Osaka Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Isao Tamai|year=1989|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|Not directly cited in the article}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1958|chapter=|title=Summary of Excavation of Matsuizuka Kofun, Taishi Town|publisher=Osaka Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Akira Yamamoto|year=2007|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun and Unearthed Pottery|title=Kofun of the Late Period and Yokoguchi Sekkaku|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} * {{ill|Yokoguchi Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} == External Links == * revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Matsuizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|280px]]
House-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku (Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property)
(Relocated and exhibited at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba, Taishi Town) |alias = |affiliation = {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |latitude_deg = 34|latitude_min = 31|latitude_sec = 08.7 |longitude_deg = 135|longitude_min = 38|longitude_sec = 25.2 |ISO = JP-27 |type = (Presumed) {{ill|Square burial mound|ja|方墳}} |size = |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}
(With house-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku inside) |grave_goods = Human remains, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |satellite = |construction_period = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_designation = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Stone coffin (Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_icon = Square burial mound |label = Matsuizuka Kofun |label_position = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It was part of the {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. The stone coffin from this kofun has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The burial mound itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|ja|昭和33年}} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s shape is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新}}, as its scale corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Yokoguchi sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}. The stone coffin was designated as an Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|ja|叡福寺}}. File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Sekkaku (Side view) File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Sekkaku interior File:松井塚古墳出土_土師器.JPG|Haji ware from the sekkaku interior
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|lt=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Matsuizuka Kofun Stone Coffin (Archaeological Material) - Designated on March 30, 1973[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (from Taishi Town section).. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * On-site explanation board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系]] 28: Osaka Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Isao Tamai|year=1989|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|Not directly cited in the article}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1958|chapter=|title=Summary of Excavation of Matsuizuka Kofun, Taishi Town|publisher=Osaka Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Akira Yamamoto|year=2007|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun and Unearthed Pottery|title=Kofun of the Late Period and Yokoguchi Sekkaku|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} * {{ill|Yokoguchi Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} == External Links == * House-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku (Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property)
(Relocated and exhibited at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba, Taishi Town) |alias = |affiliation = {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |latitude_deg = 34|latitude_min = 31|latitude_sec = 08.7 |longitude_deg = 135|longitude_min = 38|longitude_sec = 25.2 |ISO = JP-27 |type = (Presumed) {{ill|Square burial mound|ja|方墳}} |size = |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}
(With house-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku inside) |grave_goods = Human remains, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |satellite = |construction_period = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_designation = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Stone coffin (Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_icon = Square burial mound |label = Matsuizuka Kofun |label_position = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It was part of the {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. The stone coffin from this kofun has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The burial mound itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|ja|昭和33年}} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s shape is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新}}, as its scale corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Yokoguchi sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}. The stone coffin was designated as an Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|ja|叡福寺}}. File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Sekkaku (Side view) File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Sekkaku interior File:松井塚古墳出土_土師器.JPG|Haji ware from the sekkaku interior
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|lt=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Matsuizuka Kofun Stone Coffin (Archaeological Material) - Designated on March 30, 1973[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (from Taishi Town section).. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * On-site explanation board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系]] 28: Osaka Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Isao Tamai|year=1989|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|Not directly cited in the article}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1958|chapter=|title=Summary of Excavation of Matsuizuka Kofun, Taishi Town|publisher=Osaka Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Akira Yamamoto|year=2007|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun and Unearthed Pottery|title=Kofun of the Late Period and Yokoguchi Sekkaku|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} * {{ill|Yokoguchi Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} == External Links == * It was part of the {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. The stone coffin from this kofun has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The burial mound itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|ja|昭和33年}} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s shape is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新}}, as its scale corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Yokoguchi sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}. The stone coffin was designated as an Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|ja|叡福寺}}. File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Sekkaku (Side view) File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Sekkaku interior File:松井塚古墳出土_土師器.JPG|Haji ware from the sekkaku interior
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|lt=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Matsuizuka Kofun Stone Coffin (Archaeological Material) - Designated on March 30, 1973[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (from Taishi Town section).. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Akira Yamamoto|year=2007|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun and Unearthed Pottery|title=Kofun of the Late Period and Yokoguchi (With house-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku inside) |grave_goods = Human remains, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |satellite = |construction_period = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_designation = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Stone coffin (Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_icon = Square burial mound |label = Matsuizuka Kofun |label_position = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It was part of the {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. The stone coffin from this kofun has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The burial mound itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新}}, as its scale corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Yokoguchi sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}. The stone coffin was designated as an Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|ja|叡福寺}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新}}, as its scale corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Yokoguchi sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}. The stone coffin was designated as an Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|ja|叡福寺}}. {{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|lt=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture Designated revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Matsuizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|280px]]
House-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku (Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property)
(Relocated and exhibited at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba, Taishi Town) |alias = |affiliation = {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |latitude_deg = 34|latitude_min = 31|latitude_sec = 08.7 |longitude_deg = 135|longitude_min = 38|longitude_sec = 25.2 |ISO = JP-27 |type = (Presumed) {{ill|Square burial mound|ja|方墳}} |size = |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(With house-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku inside) |grave_goods = Human remains, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |satellite = |construction_period = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_designation = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Stone coffin (Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_icon = Square burial mound |label = Matsuizuka Kofun |label_position = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It was part of the {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. The stone coffin from this kofun has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The burial mound itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|ja|昭和33年}} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s shape is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新}}, as its scale corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Yokoguchi sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}}. The stone coffin was designated as an Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|ja|叡福寺}}. File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Sekkaku (Side view) File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Sekkaku interior File:松井塚古墳出土_土師器.JPG|Haji ware from the sekkaku interior
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|lt=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Matsuizuka Kofun Stone Coffin (Archaeological Material) - Designated on March 30, 1973[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (from Taishi Town section).. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系]] 28: Osaka Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Isao Tamai|year=1989|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|Not directly cited in the article}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1958|chapter=|title=Summary of Excavation of Matsuizuka Kofun, Taishi Town|publisher=Osaka Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Akira Yamamoto|year=2007|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun and Unearthed Pottery|title=Kofun of the Late Period and Yokoguchi Sekkaku|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} * {{ill|Yokoguchi Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨}} == External Links == * (Relocated and exhibited at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba, Taishi Town) |alias = |affiliation = {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |latitude_deg = 34|latitude_min = 31|latitude_sec = 08.7 |longitude_deg = 135|longitude_min = 38|longitude_sec = 25.2 |ISO = JP-27 |type = (Presumed) {{ill|Square burial mound|ja|方墳}} |size = |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(With house-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku inside) |grave_goods = Human remains, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |satellite = |construction_period = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_designation = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Stone coffin (Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_icon = Square burial mound |label = Matsuizuka Kofun |label_position = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It was part of the {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. The stone coffin from this kofun has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The burial mound itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新}}, as its scale corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Yokoguchi sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}. The stone coffin was designated as an Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|ja|叡福寺}}. {{Infobox ancient site |name = Matsuizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|280px]]
House-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku (Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property)
(Relocated and exhibited at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba, Taishi Town) |alias = |affiliation = {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |latitude_deg = 34|latitude_min = 31|latitude_sec = 08.7 |longitude_deg = 135|longitude_min = 38|longitude_sec = 25.2 |ISO = JP-27 |type = (Presumed) {{ill|Square burial mound|ja|方墳}} |size = |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(With house-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku inside) |grave_goods = Human remains, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |satellite = |construction_period = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_designation = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Stone coffin (Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_icon = Square burial mound |label = Matsuizuka Kofun |label_position = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It was part of the {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. The stone coffin from this kofun has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The burial mound itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|ja|昭和33年}} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s shape is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新}}, as its scale corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Yokoguchi sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}. The stone coffin was designated as an Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|ja|叡福寺}}. {{Infobox ancient site |name = Matsuizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|280px]]
House-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku (Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property)
(Relocated and exhibited at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba, Taishi Town) |alias = |affiliation = {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |latitude_deg = 34|latitude_min = 31|latitude_sec = 08.7 |longitude_deg = 135|longitude_min = 38|longitude_sec = 25.2 |ISO = JP-27 |type = (Presumed) {{ill|Square burial mound|ja|方墳}} |size = |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(With house-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku inside) |grave_goods = Human remains, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |satellite = |construction_period = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_designation = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Stone coffin (Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_icon = Square burial mound |label = Matsuizuka Kofun |label_position = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It was part of the {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. The stone coffin from this kofun has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The burial mound itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|ja|昭和33年}} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s shape is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新}}, as its scale corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Yokoguchi sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}. The stone coffin was designated as an Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|ja|叡福寺}}. File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Sekkaku (Side view) File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Sekkaku interior File:松井塚古墳出土_土師器.JPG|Haji ware from the sekkaku interior

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Matsuizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|280px]]
House-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku (Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property)
(Relocated and exhibited at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba, Taishi Town) |alias = |affiliation = {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |latitude_deg = 34|latitude_min = 31|latitude_sec = 08.7 |longitude_deg = 135|longitude_min = 38|longitude_sec = 25.2 |ISO = JP-27 |type = (Presumed) {{ill|Square burial mound|ja|方墳}} |size = |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(With house-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku inside) |grave_goods = Human remains, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |satellite = |construction_period = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_designation = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Stone coffin (Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_icon = Square burial mound |label = Matsuizuka Kofun |label_position = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It was part of the {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. The stone coffin from this kofun has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The burial mound itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|ja|昭和33年}} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s shape is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新}}, as its scale corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Yokoguchi sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}. The stone coffin was designated as an Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|ja|叡福寺}}. File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Sekkaku (Side view) File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Sekkaku interior File:松井塚古墳出土_土師器.JPG|Haji ware from the sekkaku interior
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|lt=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture Designated {{Infobox ancient site |name = Matsuizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|280px]]
House-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku (Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property)
(Relocated and exhibited at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba, Taishi Town) |alias = |affiliation = {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |latitude_deg = 34|latitude_min = 31|latitude_sec = 08.7 |longitude_deg = 135|longitude_min = 38|longitude_sec = 25.2 |ISO = JP-27 |type = (Presumed) {{ill|Square burial mound|ja|方墳}} |size = |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(With house-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku inside) |grave_goods = Human remains, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |satellite = |construction_period = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_designation = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Stone coffin (Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_icon = Square burial mound |label = Matsuizuka Kofun |label_position = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It was part of the {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. The stone coffin from this kofun has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The burial mound itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|ja|昭和33年}} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s shape is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新}}, as its scale corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Yokoguchi sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}. The stone coffin was designated as an Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|ja|叡福寺}}. File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Sekkaku (Side view) File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Sekkaku interior File:松井塚古墳出土_土師器.JPG|Haji ware from the sekkaku interior
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|lt=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture Designated {{Infobox ancient site |name = Matsuizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|280px]]
House-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku (Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property)
(Relocated and exhibited at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba, Taishi Town) |alias = |affiliation = {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |latitude_deg = 34|latitude_min = 31|latitude_sec = 08.7 |longitude_deg = 135|longitude_min = 38|longitude_sec = 25.2 |ISO = JP-27 |type = (Presumed) {{ill|Square burial mound|ja|方墳}} |size = |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(With house-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku inside) |grave_goods = Human remains, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |satellite = |construction_period = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_designation = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Stone coffin (Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_icon = Square burial mound |label = Matsuizuka Kofun |label_position = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It was part of the {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. The stone coffin from this kofun has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The burial mound itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|ja|昭和33年}} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s shape is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新}}, as its scale corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Yokoguchi sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}. The stone coffin was designated as an Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|ja|叡福寺}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Yokoguchi sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}. The stone coffin was designated as an Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|ja|叡福寺}}. {{Infobox ancient site |name = Matsuizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|280px]]
House-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku (Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property)
(Relocated and exhibited at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba, Taishi Town) |alias = |affiliation = {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |latitude_deg = 34|latitude_min = 31|latitude_sec = 08.7 |longitude_deg = 135|longitude_min = 38|longitude_sec = 25.2 |ISO = JP-27 |type = (Presumed) {{ill|Square burial mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} |size = |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(With house-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku inside) |grave_goods = Human remains, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |satellite = |construction_period = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_designation = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Stone coffin (Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_icon = Square burial mound |label = Matsuizuka Kofun |label_position = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It was part of the {{ill|Isonokami Burial Mounds|ja|磯長谷古墳群}}. The stone coffin from this kofun has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The burial mound itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|ja|昭和33年}} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s shape is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新}}, as its scale corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Yokoguchi sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}. The stone coffin was designated as an Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|ja|叡福寺}}.
