House-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku (Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1)
(Relocated and exhibited at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba, Taishi Town) |alternate_name = |affiliation = {{ill|Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} |location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 31|coordinates = 08.7 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 38|coordinates = 25.2 |omitted = JP-27 |type = (Presumed) {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} |size = |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(With house-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku inside) |burial goods = Human remains, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |satellite = |built = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_Historic Site = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Stone coffin (Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_type = Square Kofun |map_dot_label = Matsuizuka Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It was part of the {{ill|Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}}. The stone coffin from this kofun has been Historic Site1 as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The Kofun itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|ja|昭和33rd year }} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s type is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Retypes|ja|大化改新}}, as its map_size corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Cave Tomb sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}. The stone coffin was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48th year }} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|ja|叡福寺}}. File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Sekkaku (Side view) File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Sekkaku interior File:松井塚古墳出土_土師器.JPG|Haji pottery from the sekkaku interior

Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Retypes|ja|大化改新}}, as its map_size corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Cave Tomb sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}. The stone coffin was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48th year }} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|ja|叡福寺}}. File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Sekkaku (Side view) File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Sekkaku interior File:松井塚古墳出土_土師器.JPG|Haji pottery from the sekkaku interior
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|lt=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}.}} == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Matsuizuka Kofun Stone Coffin (Archaeological Material) - Designated on March 30, 1973[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Historic Site in Osaka Prefecture] (from Taishi Town section).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * On-site explanation board Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Isao Tamai|year=1989|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|Not directly revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} |name = Matsuizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|280px]]
House-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku (Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1)
(Relocated and exhibited at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba, Taishi Town) |alternate_name = |affiliation = {{ill|Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} |location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 31|coordinates = 08.7 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 38|coordinates = 25.2 |omitted = JP-27 |type = (Presumed) {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} |size = |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(With house-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku inside) |burial goods = Human remains, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |satellite = |built = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_Historic Site = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Stone coffin (Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_type = Square Kofun |map_dot_label = Matsuizuka Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It was part of the {{ill|Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}}. The stone coffin from this kofun has been Historic Site1 as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The Kofun itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|ja|昭和33rd year }} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s type is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Retypes|ja|大化改新}}, as its map_size corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Cave Tomb sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}. The stone coffin was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48th year }} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|ja|叡福寺}}. File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Sekkaku (Side view) File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Sekkaku interior File:松井塚古墳出土_土師器.JPG|Haji pottery from the sekkaku interior
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|lt=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}.}} == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Matsuizuka Kofun Stone Coffin (Archaeological Material) - Designated on March 30, 1973[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Historic Site in Osaka Prefecture] (from Taishi Town section).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * On-site explanation board Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Isao Tamai|year=1989|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun]]|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|Not directly cited in the article}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1958|chapter=|title=Summary of Excavation of Matsuizuka Kofun, Taishi Town|publisher=Osaka Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Akira Yamamoto|year=2007|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun and Unearthed Pottery|title=Kofun of the Late built and Cave Tomb Sekkaku|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} * {{ill|Cave Tomb House-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku (Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1)
(Relocated and exhibited at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba, Taishi Town) |alternate_name = |affiliation = {{ill|Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} |location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 31|coordinates = 08.7 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 38|coordinates = 25.2 |omitted = JP-27 |type = (Presumed) {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} |size = |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(With house-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku inside) |burial goods = Human remains, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |satellite = |built = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_Historic Site = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Stone coffin (Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_type = Square Kofun |map_dot_label = Matsuizuka Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It was part of the {{ill|Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}}. The stone coffin from this kofun has been Historic Site1 as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The Kofun itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|ja|昭和33rd year }} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s type is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Retypes|ja|大化改新}}, as its map_size corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Cave Tomb sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}. The stone coffin was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48th year }} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|ja|叡福寺}}. revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} |name = Matsuizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|280px]]
House-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku (Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1)
(Relocated and exhibited at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba, Taishi Town) |alternate_name = |affiliation = {{ill|Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} |location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 31|coordinates = 08.7 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 38|coordinates = 25.2 |omitted = JP-27 |type = (Presumed) {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} |size = |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(With house-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku inside) |burial goods = Human remains, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |satellite = |built = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_Historic Site = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Stone coffin (Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_type = Square Kofun |map_dot_label = Matsuizuka Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It was part of the {{ill|Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}}. The stone coffin from this kofun has been Historic Site1 as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The Kofun itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|ja|昭和33rd year }} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s type is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Retypes|ja|大化改新}}, as its map_size corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Cave Tomb sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}. The stone coffin was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48th year }} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|ja|叡福寺}}. File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Sekkaku (Side view) File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Sekkaku interior File:松井塚古墳出土_土師器.JPG|Haji pottery from the sekkaku interior
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|lt=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}.}} == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Matsuizuka Kofun Stone Coffin (Archaeological Material) - Designated on March 30, 1973[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Historic Site in Osaka Prefecture] (from Taishi Town section).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * On-site explanation board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系]] 28: Osaka Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Isao Tamai|year=1989|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun]]|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|Not directly cited in the article}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1958|chapter=|title=Summary of Excavation of Matsuizuka Kofun, Taishi Town|publisher=Osaka Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Akira Yamamoto|year=2007|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun and Unearthed Pottery|title=Kofun of the Late built and Cave Tomb Sekkaku|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group}} * {{ill|Cave Tomb Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] - Taishi Town Website {{citation needed|Matsuitsuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Isonokami Valley Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Taishi, Osaka|History]] [[Category:Isonokami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:History of Taishi Town, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Matsuizuka Kofun]] [[Category:Isonokami Valley Tumuli]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Matsuizuka Kofun]] [[ja:松井塚古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Matsuizuka Kofun}}

(With house-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku inside) |burial goods = Human remains, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} |satellite = |built = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_Historic Site = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Stone coffin (Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_type = Square Kofun |map_dot_label = Matsuizuka Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}. It was part of the {{ill|Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|12=draft}}. The stone coffin from this kofun has been Historic Site1 as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The Kofun itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|12=draft}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|ja|昭和33rd year|12=draft}} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s type is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺|12=draft}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|12=draft}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=draft}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=draft}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|12=draft}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Retypes|ja|大化改新|12=draft}}, as its map_size corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Cave Tomb sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}. The stone coffin was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48th year|12=draft}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|ja|叡福寺|12=draft}}. File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Sekkaku (Side view) File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Sekkaku interior File:松井塚古墳出土_土師器.JPG|Haji pottery from the sekkaku interior
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|lt=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|12=draft}}.}} == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Matsuizuka Kofun Stone Coffin (Archaeological Material) - Designated on March 30, 1973[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Historic Site in Osaka Prefecture] (from Taishi Town section).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * On-site explanation board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系]] 28: Osaka Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Isao Tamai|year=1989|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun]]|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=draft}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|Not directly cited in the article}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1958|chapter=|title=Summary of Excavation of Matsuizuka Kofun, Taishi Town|publisher=Osaka Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Akira Yamamoto|year=2007|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun and Unearthed Pottery|title=Kofun of the Late built and Cave Tomb Sekkaku|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft}}|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|12=draft}} * {{ill|Cave Tomb Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] - Taishi Town Website {{citation needed|Matsuitsuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Isonokami Valley Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Taishi, Osaka|History]] [[Category:Isonokami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:History of Taishi Town, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Matsuizuka Kofun]] [[Category:Isonokami Valley Tumuli]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Matsuizuka Kofun]] [[ja:松井塚古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Matsuizuka Kofun}}

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. House-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku (Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1)
(Relocated and exhibited at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba, Taishi Town) |alternate_name = |affiliation = {{ill|Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|12=draft}} |location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}} |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 31|coordinates = 08.7 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 38|coordinates = 25.2 |omitted = JP-27 |type = (Presumed) {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} |size = |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}
(With house-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku inside) |burial goods = Human remains, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} |satellite = |built = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_Historic Site = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Stone coffin (Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_type = Square Kofun |map_dot_label = Matsuizuka Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}. It was part of the {{ill|Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|12=draft}}. The stone coffin from this kofun has been Historic Site1 as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The Kofun itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|12=draft}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|ja|昭和33rd year|12=draft}} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s type is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺|12=draft}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|12=draft}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=draft}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=draft}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|12=draft}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Retypes|ja|大化改新|12=draft}}, as its map_size corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Cave Tomb sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}. The stone coffin was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48th year|12=draft}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|ja|叡福寺|12=draft}}. House-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku (Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1)
(Relocated and exhibited at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba, Taishi Town) |alternate_name = |affiliation = {{ill|Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|12=draft}} |location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}} |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 31|coordinates = 08.7 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 38|coordinates = 25.2 |omitted = JP-27 |type = (Presumed) {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} |size = |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}
(With house-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku inside) |burial goods = Human remains, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} |satellite = |built = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_Historic Site = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Stone coffin (Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_type = Square Kofun |map_dot_label = Matsuizuka Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}. It was part of the {{ill|Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|12=draft}}. The stone coffin from this kofun has been Historic Site1 as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The Kofun itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|12=draft}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|ja|昭和33rd year|12=draft}} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s type is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺|12=draft}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|12=draft}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=draft}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=draft}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|12=draft}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Retypes|ja|大化改新|12=draft}}, as its map_size corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Cave Tomb sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}. The stone coffin was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48th year|12=draft}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|ja|叡福寺|12=draft}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=draft}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|12=draft}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Retypes|ja|大化改新|12=draft}}, as its map_size corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Cave Tomb sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}. The stone coffin was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48th year|12=draft}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|ja|叡福寺|12=draft}}. (Relocated and exhibited at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba, Taishi Town) |alternate_name = |affiliation = {{ill|Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|12=draft}} |location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}} |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 31|coordinates = 08.7 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 38|coordinates = 25.2 |omitted = JP-27 |type = (Presumed) {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} |size = |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}
(With house-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku inside) |burial goods = Human remains, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} |satellite = |built = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_Historic Site = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Stone coffin (Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_type = Square Kofun |map_dot_label = Matsuizuka Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}. It was part of the {{ill|Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|12=draft}}. The stone coffin from this kofun has been Historic Site1 as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The Kofun itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|12=draft}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|ja|昭和33rd year|12=draft}} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s type is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺|12=draft}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|12=draft}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=draft}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=draft}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|12=draft}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Retypes|ja|大化改新|12=draft}}, as its map_size corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Cave Tomb sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}. The stone coffin was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48th year|12=draft}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|ja|叡福寺|12=draft}}. House-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku (Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1)
(Relocated and exhibited at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba, Taishi Town) |alternate_name = |affiliation = {{ill|Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|12=draft}} |location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}} |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 31|coordinates = 08.7 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 38|coordinates = 25.2 |omitted = JP-27 |type = (Presumed) {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} |size = |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}
(With house-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku inside) |burial goods = Human remains, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} |satellite = |built = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_Historic Site = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Stone coffin (Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_type = Square Kofun |map_dot_label = Matsuizuka Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}. It was part of the {{ill|Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|12=draft}}. The stone coffin from this kofun has been Historic Site1 as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The Kofun itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|12=draft}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|ja|昭和33rd year|12=draft}} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s type is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺|12=draft}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|12=draft}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=draft}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=draft}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|12=draft}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Retypes|ja|大化改新|12=draft}}, as its map_size corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Cave Tomb sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}. The stone coffin was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48th year|12=draft}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|ja|叡福寺|12=draft}}. (Relocated and exhibited at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba, Taishi Town) |alternate_name = |affiliation = {{ill|Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|12=draft}} |location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}} |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 31|coordinates = 08.7 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 38|coordinates = 25.2 |omitted = JP-27 |type = (Presumed) {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} |size = |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}
(With house-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku inside) |burial goods = Human remains, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} |satellite = |built = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_Historic Site = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Stone coffin (Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_type = Square Kofun |map_dot_label = Matsuizuka Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}. It was part of the {{ill|Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|12=draft}}. The stone coffin from this kofun has been Historic Site1 as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The Kofun itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|12=draft}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|ja|昭和33rd year|12=draft}} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s type is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺|12=draft}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|12=draft}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=draft}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=draft}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|12=draft}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Retypes|ja|大化改新|12=draft}}, as its map_size corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Cave Tomb sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}. The stone coffin was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48th year|12=draft}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|ja|叡福寺|12=draft}}. File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Sekkaku (Side view) File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Sekkaku interior File:松井塚古墳出土_土師器.JPG|Haji pottery from the sekkaku interior
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|lt=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|12=draft}}.}} == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Matsuizuka Kofun Stone Coffin (Archaeological Material) - Designated on March 30, 1973[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Historic Site in Osaka Prefecture] (from Taishi Town section).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * On-site explanation board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系]] 28: Osaka Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Isao Tamai|year=1989|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun]]|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=draft}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|Not directly cited in the article}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1958|chapter=|title=Summary of Excavation of Matsuizuka Kofun, Taishi Town|publisher=Osaka Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Akira Yamamoto|year=2007|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun and Unearthed Pottery|title=Kofun of the Late built and Cave Tomb Sekkaku|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft}}|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|12=draft}} * {{ill|Cave Tomb Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] - The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=draft}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=draft}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|12=draft}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Retypes|ja|大化改新|12=draft}}, as its map_size corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Cave Tomb sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}. The stone coffin was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48th year|12=draft}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|ja|叡福寺|12=draft}}. File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Sekkaku (Side view) File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Sekkaku interior File:松井塚古墳出土_土師器.JPG|Haji pottery from the sekkaku interior
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|lt=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|12=draft}}.}} == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Matsuizuka Kofun Stone Coffin (Archaeological Material) - Designated on March 30, 1973[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Historic Site in Osaka Prefecture] (from Taishi Town section).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * On-site explanation board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系]] 28: Osaka Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Isao Tamai|year=1989|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun]]|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=draft}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|Not directly cited in the article}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1958|chapter=|title=Summary of Excavation of Matsuizuka Kofun, Taishi Town|publisher=Osaka Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Akira Yamamoto|year=2007|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun and Unearthed Pottery|title=Kofun of the Late built and Cave Tomb Sekkaku|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft}}|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|12=draft}} * {{ill|Cave Tomb Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] - revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} |name = Matsuizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|280px]]
House-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku (Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1)
(Relocated and exhibited at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba, Taishi Town) |alternate_name = |affiliation = {{ill|Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|12=draft}} |location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}} |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 31|coordinates = 08.7 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 38|coordinates = 25.2 |omitted = JP-27 |type = (Presumed) {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} |size = |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}
(With house-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku inside) |burial goods = Human remains, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} |satellite = |built = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_Historic Site = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Stone coffin (Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_type = Square Kofun |map_dot_label = Matsuizuka Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}. It was part of the {{ill|Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|12=draft}}. The stone coffin from this kofun has been Historic Site1 as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The Kofun itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|12=draft}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|ja|昭和33rd year|12=draft}} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s type is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺|12=draft}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|12=draft}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=draft}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=draft}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|12=draft}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Retypes|ja|大化改新|12=draft}}, as its map_size corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Cave Tomb sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}. The stone coffin was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48th year|12=draft}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|ja|叡福寺|12=draft}}. File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Sekkaku (Side view) File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Sekkaku interior File:松井塚古墳出土_土師器.JPG|Haji pottery from the sekkaku interior
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|lt=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|12=draft}}.}} == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Matsuizuka Kofun Stone Coffin (Archaeological Material) - Designated on March 30, 1973[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Historic Site in Osaka Prefecture] (from Taishi Town section).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * On-site explanation board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系]] 28: Osaka Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Isao Tamai|year=1989|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun]]|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=draft}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|Not directly cited in the article}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1958|chapter=|title=Summary of Excavation of Matsuizuka Kofun, Taishi Town|publisher=Osaka Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Akira Yamamoto|year=2007|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun and Unearthed Pottery|title=Kofun of the Late built and Cave Tomb Sekkaku|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft}}|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Isonokami Valley Kofun Group|12=draft}} * {{ill|Cave Tomb Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] - Taishi Town Website {{citation needed|Matsuitsuka Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Matsuizuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Matsuizuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Isonokami Valley Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Taishi, Osaka|History]] [[Category:Isonokami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:History of Taishi Town, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Matsuizuka Kofun]] [[Category:Isonokami Valley Tumuli]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Matsuizuka Kofun]] [[ja:松井塚古墳]] {{citation needed|Matsuizuka Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}
