revision.comment: Text replacement - "Isonokami Valley Kofun Group" to "Shinga Valley Kofuns"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} |name = Matsuizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|280px]]
House-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku (Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1)
(Relocated and exhibited at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba, Taishi Town) |alternate_name = |affiliation = {{ill|Shinga Valley Kofuns|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinga Valley Kofuns|12=draft}} |location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}} |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 31|coordinates = 08.7 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 38|coordinates = 25.2 |omitted = JP-27 |type = (Presumed) {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} |size = |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}
(With house-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku inside) |burial goods = Human remains, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} |satellite = |built = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_Historic Site = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Stone coffin (Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_type = Square Kofun |map_dot_label = Matsuizuka Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}. It was part of the {{ill|Shinga Valley Kofuns|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinga Valley Kofuns|12=draft}}. The stone coffin from this kofun has been Historic Site1 as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The Kofun itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|12=draft}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|ja|昭和33rd year|12=draft}} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s type is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺|12=draft}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|12=draft}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=draft}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=draft}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|12=draft}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Retypes|ja|大化改新|12=draft}}, as its map_size corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Cave Tomb sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}. The stone coffin was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48th year|12=draft}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|ja|叡福寺|12=draft}}. File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Sekkaku (Side view) File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Sekkaku interior File:松井塚古墳出土_土師器.JPG|Haji pottery from the sekkaku interior
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|lt=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|12=draft}}.}} == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Matsuizuka Kofun Stone Coffin (Archaeological Material) - Designated on March 30, 1973[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Historic Site in Osaka Prefecture] (from Taishi Town section).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * On-site explanation board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系]] 28: Osaka Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Isao Tamai|year=1989|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun]]|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=draft}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|Not directly cited in the article}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1958|chapter=|title=Summary of Excavation of Matsuizuka Kofun, Taishi Town|publisher=Osaka Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Akira Yamamoto|year=2007|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun and Unearthed Pottery|title=Kofun of the Late built and Cave Tomb Sekkaku|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft}}|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shinga Valley Kofuns|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinga Valley Kofuns|12=draft}} * {{ill|Cave Tomb Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] - Taishi Town Website {{citation needed|Matsuitsuka Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Matsuizuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Matsuizuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Shinga Valley Kofuns]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Taishi, Osaka|History]] [[Category:Isonokami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:History of Taishi Town, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Matsuizuka Kofun]] [[Category:Isonokami Valley Tumuli]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Matsuizuka Kofun]] [[ja:松井塚古墳]] {{citation needed|Matsuizuka Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|12=draft}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=draft}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=draft}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|12=draft}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Retypes|ja|大化改新|12=draft}}, as its map_size corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Cave Tomb sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}. The stone coffin was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48th year|12=draft}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|ja|叡福寺|12=draft}}. File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Sekkaku (Side view) File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Sekkaku interior File:松井塚古墳出土_土師器.JPG|Haji pottery from the sekkaku interior
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|lt=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|12=draft}}.}} == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Matsuizuka Kofun Stone Coffin (Archaeological Material) - Designated on March 30, 1973[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Historic Site in Osaka Prefecture] (from Taishi Town section).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * On-site explanation board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系]] 28: Osaka Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Isao Tamai|year=1989|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun]]|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=draft}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|Not directly cited in the article}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1958|chapter=|title=Summary of Excavation of Matsuizuka Kofun, Taishi Town|publisher=Osaka Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Akira Yamamoto|year=2007|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun and Unearthed Pottery|title=Kofun of the Late built and Cave Tomb Sekkaku|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft}}|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=draft}} * {{ill|Cave Tomb Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] - Taishi Town Website {{citation needed|Matsuitsuka Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Matsuizuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Matsuizuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Shinagadani Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Taishi, Osaka|History]] [[Category:Isonokami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:History of Taishi Town, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Matsuizuka Kofun]] [[Category:Isonokami Valley Tumuli]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Matsuizuka Kofun]] [[ja:松井塚古墳]] {{citation needed|Matsuizuka Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Matsuizuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{Kofun navbar}}

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} |name = Matsuizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|280px]]
House-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku (Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1)
(Relocated and exhibited at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba, Taishi Town) |alternate_name = |affiliation = {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=draft}} |location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}} |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 31|coordinates = 08.7 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 38|coordinates = 25.2 |omitted = JP-27 |type = (Presumed) {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} |size = |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}
(With house-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku inside) |burial goods = Human remains, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} |satellite = |built = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_Historic Site = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Stone coffin (Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_type = Square Kofun |map_dot_label = Matsuizuka Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}. It was part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=draft}}. The stone coffin from this kofun has been Historic Site1 as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The Kofun itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|12=draft}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|ja|昭和33rd year|12=draft}} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s type is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺|12=draft}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|12=draft}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=draft}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=draft}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|12=draft}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Retypes|ja|大化改新|12=draft}}, as its map_size corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Cave Tomb sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}. The stone coffin was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48th year|12=draft}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|ja|叡福寺|12=draft}}. File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Sekkaku (Side view) File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Sekkaku interior File:松井塚古墳出土_土師器.JPG|Haji pottery from the sekkaku interior
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|lt=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|12=draft}}.}} == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Matsuizuka Kofun Stone Coffin (Archaeological Material) - Designated on March 30, 1973[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Historic Site in Osaka Prefecture] (from Taishi Town section).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * On-site explanation board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系]] 28: Osaka Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Isao Tamai|year=1989|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun]]|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=draft}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|Not directly cited in the article}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1958|chapter=|title=Summary of Excavation of Matsuizuka Kofun, Taishi Town|publisher=Osaka Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Akira Yamamoto|year=2007|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun and Unearthed Pottery|title=Kofun of the Late built and Cave Tomb Sekkaku|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft}}|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=draft}} * {{ill|Cave Tomb Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] - revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 松井塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|280px]]
家形石棺形石槨（大阪府指定文化財）
（太子町立太子・和みの広場に移築・展示） |別名 = |所属 = [[磯長谷古墳群]] |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]山田 | 緯度度 = 34| 緯度分 = 31| 緯度秒 = 08.7 | 経度度 = 135| 経度分 = 38| 経度秒 = 25.2 |ISO = JP-27 |形状 = （推定）[[方墳]] |規模 = |埋葬施設 = [[横穴式石室]]
（内部に家形石棺形石槨） |出土品 = 人骨・[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]中葉 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = 石棺（大阪府指定文化財） |特記事項 = 墳丘は非現存 |地図 = |アイコン = 方墳 |ラベル = 松井塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} '''松井塚古墳'''（まついづかこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]山田にあった[[古墳]]。[[磯長谷古墳群]]を構成した古墳の1つ。石棺は大阪府指定有形文化財に指定されている。 現在では墳丘は失われている。 == 概要 == 大阪府南東部、[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]山麓の磯長谷において、谷を形成する尾根の南斜面に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|松井塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。[[1958年]]（[[昭和]]33年）に民家の井戸掘削に伴い発見され、同年に大阪府教育委員会による発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|松井塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 墳形は明らかでないが、一説に方形と推定される{{Sfn|松井塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。埋葬施設は[[横穴式石室]]で、南方に開口し、石室の構築にあたっては[[唐尺]]の使用が推定される史跡説明板。。石室の床面には礫石が敷き詰められ、その上に[[凝灰岩]]（[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]産）製の刳抜式家形石棺形の石槨が据えられていた{{Sfn|松井塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。石槨は長さ2.5メートル・幅1.3メートルを測り{{Sfn|松井塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}、石槨前には石扉を伴う{{Sfn|松井塚古墳（平凡社）|1986}}。石槨内は盗掘に遭っており、調査では人骨（2体分か）や[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]]片のみが検出されている{{Sfn|松井塚古墳（平凡社）|1986}}。 この松井塚古墳は、[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]中葉頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|松井塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。発見当初は[[推古天皇]]の[[陪塚]]（陪冢）の可能性があるとして注目されたほか[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html 松井塚古墳の石槨]（太子町ホームページ）。、現在でも石室の規模が[[薄葬令|大化の薄葬令]]において王以上に該当する点で注目される{{Sfn|松井塚古墳（平凡社）|1986}}。また[[横口式石槨]]の変遷を考察するうえでも重要視される古墳である。 石棺は[[1973年]]（昭和48年）に大阪府指定有形文化財に指定され、現在では太子町立太子・和みの広場（[[叡福寺]]に隣接）に移築・展示されている。 Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|石槨 横景 Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|石槨内部 松井塚古墳出土 土師器.JPG|石槨内に敷かれた土師器
{{small|[[大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館]]企画展示時に撮影。}} == 文化財 == === 大阪府指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 松井塚古墳石棺（考古資料） - 1973年（昭和48年）3月30日指定[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html 大阪府内指定文化財一覧表]（大阪府ホームページ）の太子町ファイルより。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=松井塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 28 大阪府の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|松井塚古墳（平凡社）|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=玉井功|year=1989|chapter=松井塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|松井塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1958|chapter=|title=太子町松井塚古墳発掘調査概要|publisher=大阪府教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=山本彰|year=2007|chapter=松井塚古墳と出土土器|title=終末期古墳と横口式石槨|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[磯長谷古墳群]] * [[横口式石槨]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html 松井塚古墳の石槨] - 太子町ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:まついつかこふん}} [[Category:磯長谷古墳群]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:大阪府太子町の歴史]] [[Category:方墳]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 松井塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|280px]]
家形石棺形石槨（大阪府指定文化財）
（太子町立太子・和みの広場に移築・展示） |別名 = |所属 = [[磯長谷古墳群]] |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]山田 | 緯度度 = 34| 緯度分 = 31| 緯度秒 = 08.7 | 経度度 = 135| 経度分 = 38| 経度秒 = 25.2 |ISO = JP-27 |形状 = （推定）[[方墳]] |規模 = |埋葬施設 = [[横穴式石室]]
（内部に家形石棺形石槨） |出土品 = 人骨・[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]中葉 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = 石棺（大阪府指定文化財） |特記事項 = 墳丘は非現存 |地図 = |アイコン = 方墳 |ラベル = 松井塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} '''松井塚古墳'''（まついづかこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]山田にあった[[古墳]]。[[磯長谷古墳群]]を構成した古墳の1つ。石棺は大阪府指定有形文化財に指定されている。 現在では墳丘は失われている。 == 概要 == 大阪府南東部、[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]山麓の磯長谷において、谷を形成する尾根の南斜面に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|松井塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。[[1958年]]（[[昭和]]33年）に民家の井戸掘削に伴い発見され、同年に大阪府教育委員会による発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|松井塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 墳形は明らかでないが、一説に方形と推定される{{Sfn|松井塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。埋葬施設は[[横穴式石室]]で、南方に開口し、石室の構築にあたっては[[唐尺]]の使用が推定される史跡説明板。。石室の床面には礫石が敷き詰められ、その上に[[凝灰岩]]（[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]産）製の刳抜式家形石棺形の石槨が据えられていた{{Sfn|松井塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。石槨は長さ2.5メートル・幅1.3メートルを測り{{Sfn|松井塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}、石槨前には石扉を伴う{{Sfn|松井塚古墳（平凡社）|1986}}。石槨内は盗掘に遭っており、調査では人骨（2体分か）や[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]]片のみが検出されている{{Sfn|松井塚古墳（平凡社）|1986}}。 この松井塚古墳は、[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]中葉頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|松井塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。発見当初は[[推古天皇]]の[[陪塚]]（陪冢）の可能性があるとして注目されたほか[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html 松井塚古墳の石槨]（太子町ホームページ）。、現在でも石室の規模が[[薄葬令|大化の薄葬令]]において王以上に該当する点で注目される{{Sfn|松井塚古墳（平凡社）|1986}}。また[[横口式石槨]]の変遷を考察するうえでも重要視される古墳である。 石棺は[[1973年]]（昭和48年）に大阪府指定有形文化財に指定され、現在では太子町立太子・和みの広場（[[叡福寺]]に隣接）に移築・展示されている。 Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|石槨 横景 Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|石槨内部 松井塚古墳出土 土師器.JPG|石槨内に敷かれた土師器
{{small|[[大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館]]企画展示時に撮影。}} == 文化財 == === 大阪府指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 松井塚古墳石棺（考古資料） - 1973年（昭和48年）3月30日指定[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html 大阪府内指定文化財一覧表]（大阪府ホームページ）の太子町ファイルより。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=松井塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 28 大阪府の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|松井塚古墳（平凡社）|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=玉井功|year=1989|chapter=松井塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|松井塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1958|chapter=|title=太子町松井塚古墳発掘調査概要|publisher=大阪府教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=山本彰|year=2007|chapter=松井塚古墳と出土土器|title=終末期古墳と横口式石槨|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[磯長谷古墳群]] * [[横口式石槨]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html 松井塚古墳の石槨] - 太子町ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:まついつかこふん}} [[Category:磯長谷古墳群]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:大阪府太子町の歴史]] [[Category:方墳]]

{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} |name = Matsuizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|280px]]
House-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku (Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1)
(Relocated and exhibited at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba, Taishi Town) |alternate_name = |affiliation = {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |location = [[:en:Taishi, Osaka|Taishi, Osaka]], [[:en:Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 31|coordinates = 08.7 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 38|coordinates = 25.2 |omitted = JP-27 |type = (Presumed) {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} |size = |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(With house-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku inside) |burial goods = Human remains, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |satellite = |built = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_Historic Site = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Stone coffin (Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_type = Square Kofun |map_dot_label = Matsuizuka Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Taishi, Osaka|Taishi, Osaka]], [[:en:Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It was part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. The stone coffin from this kofun has been Historic Site1 as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The Kofun itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of [[:en:Mount Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|ja|昭和33年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1958|qq=}} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s type is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang|qq=}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from [[:en:Mount Nijō|Mount Nijō]]{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun period|qq=}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (accompanying tomb) of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]][http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Retypes|ja|大化改新|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taika Retypes|qq=}}, as its map_size corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Cave Tomb sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}. The stone coffin was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1973|qq=}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near [[:en:Eifuku-ji|Eifuku-ji]]. File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Sekkaku (Side view) File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Sekkaku interior File:松井塚古墳出土_土師器.JPG|Haji pottery from the sekkaku interior
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}} == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Matsuizuka Kofun Stone Coffin (Archaeological Material) - Designated on March 30, 1973[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Historic Site in Osaka Prefecture] (from Taishi Town section).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * On-site explanation board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系]] 28: Osaka Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Isao Tamai|year=1989|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun]]|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo-do Publishing|qq=}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|Not directly cited in the article}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1958|chapter=|title=Summary of Excavation of Matsuizuka Kofun, Taishi Town|publisher=Osaka Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Akira Yamamoto|year=2007|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun and Unearthed Pottery|title=Kofun of the Late built and Cave Tomb Sekkaku|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * {{ill|Cave Tomb Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] - Taishi Town Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|松井塚古墳|version=96954926|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Shinagadani Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Taishi, Osaka|History]] [[Category:Isonokami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:History of Taishi Town, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Isonokami Valley Tumuli]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Matsuizuka Kofun]] [[ja:松井塚古墳]]

(With house-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku inside) |burial goods = Human remains, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |satellite = |built = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_Historic Site = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Stone coffin (Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_type = Square Kofun |map_dot_label = Matsuizuka Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Taishi, Osaka|Taishi, Osaka]], [[:en:Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It was part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. The stone coffin from this kofun has been Historic Site1 as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The Kofun itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of [[:en:Mount Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|ja|昭和33年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1958|qq=}} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s type is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang|qq=}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from [[:en:Mount Nijō|Mount Nijō]]{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun period|qq=}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (accompanying tomb) of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]][http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Retypes|ja|大化改新|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taika Retypes|qq=}}, as its map_size corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Cave Tomb sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}. The stone coffin was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1973|qq=}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near [[:en:Eifuku-ji|Eifuku-ji]]. File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Sekkaku (Side view) File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Sekkaku interior File:松井塚古墳出土_土師器.JPG|Haji pottery from the sekkaku interior
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}} == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Matsuizuka Kofun Stone Coffin (Archaeological Material) - Designated on March 30, 1973[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Historic Site in Osaka Prefecture] (from Taishi Town section).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * On-site explanation board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系]] 28: Osaka Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Isao Tamai|year=1989|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun]]|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo-do Publishing|qq=}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|Not directly cited in the article}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1958|chapter=|title=Summary of Excavation of Matsuizuka Kofun, Taishi Town|publisher=Osaka Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Akira Yamamoto|year=2007|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun and Unearthed Pottery|title=Kofun of the Late built and Cave Tomb Sekkaku|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * {{ill|Cave Tomb Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] - (With house-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku inside) |burial goods = Human remains, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |satellite = |built = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_Historic Site = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Stone coffin (Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_type = Square Kofun |map_dot_label = Matsuizuka Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Taishi, Osaka|Taishi, Osaka]], [[:en:Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It was part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. The stone coffin from this kofun has been Historic Site1 as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The Kofun itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of [[:en:Mount Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|ja|昭和33年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1958|qq=}} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang|qq=}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from [[:en:Mount Nijō|Mount Nijō]]{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (accompanying tomb) of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]][http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Retypes|ja|大化改新|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taika Retypes|qq=}}, as its map_size corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Cave Tomb sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}. Designated on March 30, 1973[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Historic Site in Osaka Prefecture] (from Taishi Town section).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * On-site explanation board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系]] 28: Osaka Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Isao Tamai|year=1989|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun]]|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo-do Publishing|qq=}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|Not directly cited in the article}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1958|chapter=|title=Summary of Excavation of Matsuizuka Kofun, Taishi Town|publisher=Osaka Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Akira Yamamoto|year=2007|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun and Unearthed Pottery|title=Kofun of the Late built and Cave Tomb Sekkaku|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * {{ill|Cave Tomb Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] - (With house-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku inside) |burial goods = Human remains, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |satellite = |built = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_Historic Site = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Stone coffin (Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_type = Square Kofun |map_dot_label = Matsuizuka Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Taishi, Osaka|Taishi, Osaka]], [[:en:Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It was part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. The stone coffin from this kofun has been Historic Site1 as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The Kofun itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of [[:en:Mount Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|ja|昭和33年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1958|qq=}} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s type is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang|qq=}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from [[:en:Mount Nijō|Mount Nijō]]{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun period|qq=}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (accompanying tomb) of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]][http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Retypes|ja|大化改新|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taika Retypes|qq=}}, as its map_size corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Cave Tomb sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}. The stone coffin was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1973|qq=}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near [[:en:Eifuku-ji|Eifuku-ji]]. File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Sekkaku (Side view) File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Sekkaku interior File:松井塚古墳出土_土師器.JPG|Haji pottery from the sekkaku interior
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}} == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Matsuizuka Kofun Stone Coffin (Archaeological Material) - Designated on March 30, 1973[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Historic Site in Osaka Prefecture] (from Taishi Town section).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * On-site explanation board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系]] 28: Osaka Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Isao Tamai|year=1989|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun]]|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo-do Publishing|qq=}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|Not directly cited in the article}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1958|chapter=|title=Summary of Excavation of Matsuizuka Kofun, Taishi Town|publisher=Osaka Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Akira Yamamoto|year=2007|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun and Unearthed Pottery|title=Kofun of the Late built and Cave Tomb Sekkaku|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * {{ill|Cave Tomb Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] - House-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku (Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1)
(Relocated and exhibited at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba, Taishi Town) |alternate_name = |affiliation = {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |location = [[:en:Taishi, Osaka|Taishi, Osaka]], [[:en:Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 31|coordinates = 08.7 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 38|coordinates = 25.2 |omitted = JP-27 |type = (Presumed) {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} |size = |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(With house-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku inside) |burial goods = Human remains, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |satellite = |built = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_Historic Site = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Stone coffin (Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_type = Square Kofun |map_dot_label = Matsuizuka Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Taishi, Osaka|Taishi, Osaka]], [[:en:Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It was part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. The stone coffin from this kofun has been Historic Site1 as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The Kofun itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of [[:en:Mount Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|ja|昭和33年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1958|qq=}} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s type is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang|qq=}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from [[:en:Mount Nijō|Mount Nijō]]{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun period|qq=}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (accompanying tomb) of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]][http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Retypes|ja|大化改新|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taika Retypes|qq=}}, as its map_size corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Cave Tomb sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}. The stone coffin was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1973|qq=}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near [[:en:Eifuku-ji|Eifuku-ji]]. File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Sekkaku (Side view) File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Sekkaku interior File:松井塚古墳出土_土師器.JPG|Haji pottery from the sekkaku interior
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}} == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Matsuizuka Kofun Stone Coffin (Archaeological Material) - Designated on March 30, 1973[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Historic Site in Osaka Prefecture] (from Taishi Town section).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * On-site explanation board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系]] 28: Osaka Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Isao Tamai|year=1989|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun]]|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo-do Publishing|qq=}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|Not directly cited in the article}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1958|chapter=|title=Summary of Excavation of Matsuizuka Kofun, Taishi Town|publisher=Osaka Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Akira Yamamoto|year=2007|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun and Unearthed Pottery|title=Kofun of the Late built and Cave Tomb Sekkaku|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * {{ill|Cave Tomb Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] - Taishi Town Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|松井塚古墳|version=96954926|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Shinagadani Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Taishi, Osaka|History]] [[Category:Isonokami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:History of Taishi Town, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Isonokami Valley Tumuli]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Matsuizuka Kofun]] [[ja:松井塚古墳]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:大阪府太子町の歴史]] [[Category:磯長谷古墳群]] {{Commons category|Matsuizuka Kofun}}

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} |name = Matsuizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|280px]]
House-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku (Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1)
(Relocated and exhibited at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba, Taishi Town) |alternate_name = |affiliation = {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |location = [[:en:Taishi, Osaka|Taishi, Osaka]], [[:en:Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 31|coordinates = 08.7 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 38|coordinates = 25.2 |omitted = JP-27 |type = (Presumed) {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} |size = |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(With house-shaped stone coffin-type sekkaku inside) |burial goods = Human remains, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |satellite = |built = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_Historic Site = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Stone coffin (Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_type = Square Kofun |map_dot_label = Matsuizuka Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Taishi, Osaka|Taishi, Osaka]], [[:en:Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It was part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. The stone coffin from this kofun has been Historic Site1 as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The Kofun itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of [[:en:Mount Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|ja|昭和33年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1958|qq=}} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s type is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang|qq=}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from [[:en:Mount Nijō|Mount Nijō]]{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun period|qq=}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (accompanying tomb) of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]][http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Retypes|ja|大化改新|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taika Retypes|qq=}}, as its map_size corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Cave Tomb sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}. The stone coffin was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1973|qq=}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near [[:en:Eifuku-ji|Eifuku-ji]]. File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Sekkaku (Side view) File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Sekkaku interior File:松井塚古墳出土_土師器.JPG|Haji pottery from the sekkaku interior
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}} == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Matsuizuka Kofun Stone Coffin (Archaeological Material) - Designated on March 30, 1973[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Historic Site in Osaka Prefecture] (from Taishi Town section).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * On-site explanation board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系]] 28: Osaka Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Isao Tamai|year=1989|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun]]|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo-do Publishing|qq=}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|Not directly cited in the article}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1958|chapter=|title=Summary of Excavation of Matsuizuka Kofun, Taishi Town|publisher=Osaka Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Akira Yamamoto|year=2007|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun and Unearthed Pottery|title=Kofun of the Late built and Cave Tomb Sekkaku|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * {{ill|Cave Tomb Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] -
