[[sn:commons:Category:Matsuizuka Kofun]][[ja:松井塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q63386720]] Wikidata name: Matsuizuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Matsuizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|280px]]
House-shaped Sarcophagus-type sekkaku (Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1)
(Relocated and exhibited at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba, Taishi Town) |alternate_name = |affiliation = {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |location = [[:en:Taishi, Osaka|Taishi, Osaka]], [[:en:Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 31|coordinates = 08.7 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 38|coordinates = 25.2 |omitted = JP-27 |type = (Presumed) {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |size = |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(With house-shaped Sarcophagus-type sekkaku inside) |burial goods = Human remains, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |satellite = |built = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_Historic Site = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Sarcophagus (Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_type = Square Kofun |map_dot_label = Matsuizuka Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Taishi, Osaka|Taishi, Osaka]], [[:en:Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It was part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. The Sarcophagus from this kofun has been Historic Site1 as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The Kofun itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of [[:en:Mount Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in [[:en:1958|Showa 33]] during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s type is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang|1=Tang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10922645}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from [[:en:Mount Nijō|Mount Nijō]]{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (accompanying tomb) of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]][http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Retypes|ja|大化改新|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taika Retypes|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大化の改新|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大化の改新|1=Taika Reform|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q570140}}, as its map_size corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Cave Tomb sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}. The Sarcophagus was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 in [[:en:1973|Showa 48]] and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near [[:en:Eifuku-ji|Eifuku-ji]]. File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Sekkaku (Side view) File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Sekkaku interior File:松井塚古墳出土_土師器.JPG|Haji pottery from the sekkaku interior
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}} == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Matsuizuka Kofun Stone Coffin (Archaeological Material) - Designated on March 30, 1973[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Historic Site in Osaka Prefecture] (from Taishi Town section).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * On-site explanation board * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系]] 28: Osaka Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Isao Tamai|year=1989|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun]]|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo-do Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|Not directly cited in the article}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1958|chapter=|title=Summary of Excavation of Matsuizuka Kofun, Taishi Town|publisher=Osaka Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Akira Yamamoto|year=2007|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun and Unearthed Pottery|title=Kofun of the Late built and Cave Tomb Sekkaku|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * {{ill|Cave Tomb Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} == External Links == * The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. House-shaped Sarcophagus-type sekkaku (Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1)
(Relocated and exhibited at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba, Taishi Town) |alternate_name = |affiliation = {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |location = [[:en:Taishi, Osaka|Taishi, Osaka]], [[:en:Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 31|coordinates = 08.7 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 38|coordinates = 25.2 |omitted = JP-27 |type = (Presumed) {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |size = |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(With house-shaped Sarcophagus-type sekkaku inside) |burial goods = Human remains, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |satellite = |built = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_Historic Site = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Sarcophagus (Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_type = Square Kofun |map_dot_label = Matsuizuka Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Taishi, Osaka|Taishi, Osaka]], [[:en:Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It was part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. The Sarcophagus from this kofun has been Historic Site1 as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The Kofun itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of [[:en:Mount Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in [[:en:1958|Showa 33]] during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s type is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang|1=Tang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10922645}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from [[:en:Mount Nijō|Mount Nijō]]{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (accompanying tomb) of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]][http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Retypes|ja|大化改新|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taika Retypes|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大化の改新|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大化の改新|1=Taika Reform|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q570140}}, as its map_size corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Cave Tomb sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}. The Sarcophagus was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 in [[:en:1973|Showa 48]] and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near [[:en:Eifuku-ji|Eifuku-ji]]. File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Sekkaku (Side view) File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Sekkaku interior File:松井塚古墳出土_土師器.JPG|Haji pottery from the sekkaku interior
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}} == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Matsuizuka Kofun Stone Coffin (Archaeological Material) - Designated on March 30, 1973[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Historic Site in Osaka Prefecture] (from Taishi Town section).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * On-site explanation board * {{Cite Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Isao Tamai|year=1989|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun]]|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo-do Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|Not directly cited in the article}} * {{Cite Yamamoto|year=2007|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun and Unearthed Pottery|title=Kofun of the Late built and Cave Tomb Sekkaku|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * {{ill|Cave Tomb Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} == External Links == * House-shaped Sarcophagus-type sekkaku (Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1)
(Relocated and exhibited at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba, Taishi Town) |alternate_name = |affiliation = {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |location = [[:en:Taishi, Osaka|Taishi, Osaka]], [[:en:Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 31|coordinates = 08.7 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 38|coordinates = 25.2 |omitted = JP-27 |type = (Presumed) {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |size = |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(With house-shaped Sarcophagus-type sekkaku inside) |burial goods = Human remains, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |satellite = |built = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_Historic Site = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Sarcophagus (Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_type = Square Kofun |map_dot_label = Matsuizuka Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Taishi, Osaka|Taishi, Osaka]], [[:en:Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It was part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. The Sarcophagus from this kofun has been Historic Site1 as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The Kofun itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of [[:en:Mount Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in [[:en:1958|Showa 33]] during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s type is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang|1=Tang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10922645}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from [[:en:Mount Nijō|Mount Nijō]]{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (accompanying tomb) of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]][http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Retypes|ja|大化改新|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taika Retypes|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大化の改新|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大化の改新|1=Taika Reform|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q570140}}, as its map_size corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Cave Tomb sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}. The Sarcophagus was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 in [[:en:1973|Showa 48]] and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near [[:en:Eifuku-ji|Eifuku-ji]]. House-shaped Sarcophagus-type sekkaku (Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1)
(Relocated and exhibited at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba, Taishi Town) |alternate_name = |affiliation = {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |location = [[:en:Taishi, Osaka|Taishi, Osaka]], [[:en:Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 31|coordinates = 08.7 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 38|coordinates = 25.2 |omitted = JP-27 |type = (Presumed) {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |size = |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(With house-shaped Sarcophagus-type sekkaku inside) |burial goods = Human remains, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |satellite = |built = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_Historic Site = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Sarcophagus (Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_type = Square Kofun |map_dot_label = Matsuizuka Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Taishi, Osaka|Taishi, Osaka]], [[:en:Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It was part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. The Sarcophagus from this kofun has been Historic Site1 as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The Kofun itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of [[:en:Mount Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in [[:en:1958|Showa 33]] during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s type is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang|1=Tang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10922645}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from [[:en:Mount Nijō|Mount Nijō]]{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (accompanying tomb) of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]][http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Retypes|ja|大化改新|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taika Retypes|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大化の改新|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大化の改新|1=Taika Reform|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q570140}}, as its map_size corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Cave Tomb sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}. The Sarcophagus was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 in [[:en:1973|Showa 48]] and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near [[:en:Eifuku-ji|Eifuku-ji]]. File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Sekkaku (Side view) File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Sekkaku interior File:松井塚古墳出土_土師器.JPG|Haji pottery from the sekkaku interior

House-shaped Sarcophagus-type sekkaku (Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1)
(Relocated and exhibited at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba, Taishi Town) |alternate_name = |affiliation = {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |location = [[:en:Taishi, Osaka|Taishi, Osaka]], [[:en:Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 31|coordinates = 08.7 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 38|coordinates = 25.2 |omitted = JP-27 |type = (Presumed) {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |size = |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(With house-shaped Sarcophagus-type sekkaku inside) |burial goods = Human remains, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |satellite = |built = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_Historic Site = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Sarcophagus (Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_type = Square Kofun |map_dot_label = Matsuizuka Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Taishi, Osaka|Taishi, Osaka]], [[:en:Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It was part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. The Sarcophagus from this kofun has been Historic Site1 as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The Kofun itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of [[:en:Mount Nijō|Mount Nijō]] in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in [[:en:1958|Showa 33]] during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s type is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang|1=Tang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10922645}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from [[:en:Mount Nijō|Mount Nijō]]{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (accompanying tomb) of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]][http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Retypes|ja|大化改新|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taika Retypes|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大化の改新|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大化の改新|1=Taika Reform|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q570140}}, as its map_size corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Cave Tomb sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}. The Sarcophagus was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 in [[:en:1973|Showa 48]] and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near [[:en:Eifuku-ji|Eifuku-ji]]. File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Sekkaku (Side view) File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Sekkaku interior File:松井塚古墳出土_土師器.JPG|Haji pottery from the sekkaku interior
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}} == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s type is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|en|Mount Nijō|WD=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Retypes|ja|大化改新|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taika Retypes|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大化の改新|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大化の改新|1=Taika Reform|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q570140}}, as its map_size corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Cave Tomb sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}. The Sarcophagus was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 in {{ill|1973|en|1973|WD=Q2477|lt=Showa 48}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|en|Eifuku-ji|WD=Q11410732|lt=Eifuku-ji}}. File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Sekkaku (Side view) File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Sekkaku interior File:松井塚古墳出土_土師器.JPG|Haji pottery from the sekkaku interior
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|en|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|WD=Q4557237|lt=Chikatsu Asuka Museum}}.}} == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Matsuizuka Kofun Stone Coffin (Archaeological Material) - Designated on March 30, 1973[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Historic Site in Osaka Prefecture] (from Taishi Town section).. == References == {{reflist}} == [[co:commons:Category:Matsuizuka Kofun]][[ja:松井塚古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Matsuizuka Kofun]][[ja:松井塚古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Matsuizuka Kofun]][[ja:松井塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q63386720]] Wikidata name: Matsuizuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Matsuizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|280px]]
House-shaped Sarcophagus-type sekkaku (Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1)
(Relocated and exhibited at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba, Taishi Town) |alternate_name = |affiliation = {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|en|Taishi, Osaka|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi, Osaka}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|en|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|WD=Q1347323|lt=Minamikawachi District, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}} |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 31|coordinates = 08.7 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 38|coordinates = 25.2 |omitted = JP-27 |type = (Presumed) {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |size = |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(With house-shaped Sarcophagus-type sekkaku inside) |burial goods = Human remains, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} |satellite = |built = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_Historic Site = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Sarcophagus (Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_type = Square Kofun |map_dot_label = Matsuizuka Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|en|Taishi, Osaka|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi, Osaka}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|en|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|WD=Q1347323|lt=Minamikawachi District, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}. It was part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. The Sarcophagus from this kofun has been Historic Site1 as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The Kofun itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijō|en|Mount Nijō|WD=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|en|1958|WD=Q5253|lt=Showa 33}} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s type is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang|1=Tang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10922645}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|en|Mount Nijō|WD=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Retypes|ja|大化改新|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taika Retypes|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大化の改新|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大化の改新|1=Taika Reform|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q570140}}, as its map_size corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Cave Tomb sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}. The Sarcophagus was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 in {{ill|1973|en|1973|WD=Q2477|lt=Showa 48}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|en|Eifuku-ji|WD=Q11410732|lt=Eifuku-ji}}. File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Sekkaku (Side view) File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Sekkaku interior File:松井塚古墳出土_土師器.JPG|Haji pottery from the sekkaku interior
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|en|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|WD=Q4557237|lt=Chikatsu Asuka Museum}}.}} == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Matsuizuka Kofun Stone Coffin (Archaeological Material) - Designated on March 30, 1973[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Historic Site in Osaka Prefecture] (from Taishi Town section).. == References == {{reflist}} == and Cave Tomb Sekkaku|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * {{ill|Cave Tomb Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb It was part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|en|1958|WD=Q5253|lt=Showa 33}} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s type is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang|1=Tang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10922645}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|en|Mount Nijō|WD=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Retypes|ja|大化改新|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taika Retypes|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大化の改新|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大化の改新|1=Taika Reform|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q570140}}, as its map_size corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Cave Tomb sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}. The Sarcophagus was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 in {{ill|1973|en|1973|WD=Q2477|lt=Showa 48}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|en|Eifuku-ji|WD=Q11410732|lt=Eifuku-ji}}. File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Sekkaku (Side view) File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Sekkaku interior File:松井塚古墳出土_土師器.JPG|Haji pottery from the sekkaku interior
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|en|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|WD=Q4557237|lt=Chikatsu Asuka Museum}}.}} == Historic Site == 1973[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Historic Site in Osaka Prefecture] (from Taishi Town section).. == References == {{reflist}} == (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|Not directly cited in the article}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1958|chapter=|title=Summary of Excavation of Matsuizuka Kofun, Taishi (Relocated and exhibited at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba, Taishi Town) |alternate_name = |affiliation = {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|en|Taishi, Osaka|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi, Osaka}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|en|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|WD=Q1347323|lt=Minamikawachi District, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}} |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 31|coordinates = 08.7 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 38|coordinates = 25.2 |omitted = JP-27 |type = (Presumed) {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |size = |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(With house-shaped Sarcophagus-type sekkaku inside) |burial goods = Human remains, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} |satellite = |built = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_Historic Site = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Sarcophagus (Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_type = Square Kofun |map_dot_label = Matsuizuka Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|en|Taishi, Osaka|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi, Osaka}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|en|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|WD=Q1347323|lt=Minamikawachi District, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}. It was part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. The Sarcophagus from this kofun has been Historic Site1 as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The Kofun itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijō|en|Mount Nijō|WD=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|en|1958|WD=Q5253|lt=Showa 33}} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s type is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang|1=Tang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10922645}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|en|Mount Nijō|WD=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Retypes|ja|大化改新|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taika Retypes|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大化の改新|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大化の改新|1=Taika Reform|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q570140}}, as its map_size corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Cave Tomb sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}. The Sarcophagus was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 in {{ill|1973|en|1973|WD=Q2477|lt=Showa 48}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|en|Eifuku-ji|WD=Q11410732|lt=Eifuku-ji}}. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s type is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang|1=Tang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10922645}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|en|Mount Nijō|WD=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Retypes|ja|大化改新|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taika Retypes|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大化の改新|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大化の改新|1=Taika Reform|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q570140}}, as its map_size corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Yokoanabo sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}. The Sarcophagus was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 in {{ill|1973|en|1973|WD=Q2477|lt=Showa 48}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|en|Eifuku-ji|WD=Q11410732|lt=Eifuku-ji}}. File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Sekkaku (Side view) File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Sekkaku interior File:松井塚古墳出土_土師器.JPG|Haji pottery from the sekkaku interior
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|en|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|WD=Q4557237|lt=Chikatsu Asuka Museum}}.}} == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Matsuizuka Kofun Stone Coffin (Archaeological Material) - Designated on March 30, 1973[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Historic Site in Osaka Prefecture] (from Taishi Town section).. == References == {{reflist}} == (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|Not directly cited in the article}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1958|chapter=|title=Summary of Excavation of Matsuizuka Kofun, Taishi Town|publisher=Osaka Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Akira Yamamoto|year=2007|chapter=Matsuizuka Kofun and Unearthed Pottery|title=Kofun of the Late built and Yokoanabo Sekkaku|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * {{ill|Yokoanabo Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo House-shaped Sarcophagus-type sekkaku (Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1)
(Relocated and exhibited at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba, Taishi Town) |alternate_name = |affiliation = {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|en|Taishi, Osaka|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi, Osaka}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|en|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|WD=Q1347323|lt=Minamikawachi District, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}} |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 31|coordinates = 08.7 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 38|coordinates = 25.2 |omitted = JP-27 |type = (Presumed) {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |size = |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(With house-shaped Sarcophagus-type sekkaku inside) |burial goods = Human remains, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} |satellite = |built = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_Historic Site = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Sarcophagus (Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_type = Square Kofun |map_dot_label = Matsuizuka Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|en|Taishi, Osaka|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi, Osaka}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|en|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|WD=Q1347323|lt=Minamikawachi District, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}. It was part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. The Sarcophagus from this kofun has been Historic Site1 as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The Kofun itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijō|en|Mount Nijō|WD=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|en|1958|WD=Q5253|lt=Showa 33}} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s type is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang|1=Tang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10922645}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|en|Mount Nijō|WD=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Retypes|ja|大化改新|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taika Retypes|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大化の改新|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大化の改新|1=Taika Reform|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q570140}}, as its map_size corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Yokoanabo sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}. The Sarcophagus was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 in {{ill|1973|en|1973|WD=Q2477|lt=Showa 48}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|en|Eifuku-ji|WD=Q11410732|lt=Eifuku-ji}}. File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Sekkaku (Side view) File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Sekkaku interior File:松井塚古墳出土_土師器.JPG|Haji pottery from the sekkaku interior
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|en|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|WD=Q4557237|lt=Chikatsu Asuka Museum}}.}} == Historic Site == Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s type is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang|1=Tang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10922645}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|en|Mount Nijō|WD=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Retypes|ja|大化改新|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taika Retypes|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大化の改新|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大化の改新|1=Taika Reform|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q570140}}, as its map_size corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Yokoanabo sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}. The Sarcophagus was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 in {{ill|1973|en|1973|WD=Q2477|lt=Showa 48}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|en|Eifuku-ji|WD=Q11410732|lt=Eifuku-ji}}. File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Sekkaku (Side view) File:Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-3.jpg|Sekkaku interior File:松井塚古墳出土_土師器.JPG|Haji pottery from the sekkaku interior
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|en|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|WD=Q4557237|lt=Chikatsu Asuka Museum}}.}} == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Matsuizuka Kofun Stone Coffin (Archaeological Material) - Designated on March 30, 1973[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Historic Site in Osaka Prefecture] (from Taishi Town section).. == References == {{reflist}} == House-shaped Sarcophagus-type sekkaku (Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1)
(Relocated and exhibited at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba, Taishi Town) |alternate_name = |affiliation = {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|en|Taishi, Osaka|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi, Osaka|1=Taishi, Osaka}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|en|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|WD=Q1347323|lt=Minamikawachi District, Osaka|1=Minamikawachi District, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}} |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 31|coordinates = 08.7 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 38|coordinates = 25.2 |omitted = JP-27 |type = (Presumed) {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |size = |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(With house-shaped Sarcophagus-type sekkaku inside) |burial goods = Human remains, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} |satellite = |built = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_Historic Site = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Sarcophagus (Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_type = Square Kofun |map_dot_label = Matsuizuka Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|en|Taishi, Osaka|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi, Osaka|1=Taishi, Osaka}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|en|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|WD=Q1347323|lt=Minamikawachi District, Osaka|1=Minamikawachi District, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It was part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. The Sarcophagus from this kofun has been Historic Site1 as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The Kofun itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijō|en|Mount Nijō|WD=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō|1=Mount Nijō}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|en|1958|WD=Q5253|lt=Showa 33|1=1958}} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s type is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang|1=Q10922645|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10922645}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|en|Mount Nijō|WD=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō|1=Mount Nijō}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko|1=Empress Suiko}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Retypes|ja|大化改新|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taika Retypes|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大化の改新|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大化の改新|1=Taika Reform|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q570140}}, as its map_size corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Yokoanabo sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}. The Sarcophagus was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 in {{ill|1973|en|1973|WD=Q2477|lt=Showa 48|1=1973}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|en|Eifuku-ji|WD=Q11410732|lt=Eifuku-ji|1=Eifuku-ji}}. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}.
