The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang|1=Q10922645|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10922645}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|en|Mount Nijō|WD=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō|1=Mount Nijō}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko|1=Empress Suiko}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Retypes|ja|大化改新|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taika Retypes|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大化の改新|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大化の改新|1=Taika Reform|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q570140}}, as its map_size corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Yokoanabo sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}. The Sarcophagus was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 in {{ill|1973|en|1973|WD=Q2477|lt=Showa 48|1=1973}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|en|Eifuku-ji|WD=Q11410732|lt=Eifuku-ji|1=Eifuku-ji}}. It was part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. The Sarcophagus from this kofun has been Historic Site1 as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The Kofun itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijō|en|Mount Nijō|WD=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō|1=Mount Nijō}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|en|Mount Nijō|WD=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō|1=Mount Nijō}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko|1=Empress Suiko}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The mound’s type is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang|1=Q10922645|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10922645}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|en|Mount Nijō|WD=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō|1=Mount Nijō}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko|1=Empress Suiko}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Retypes|ja|大化改新|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taika Retypes|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大化の改新|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大化の改新|1=Taika Reform|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q570140}}, as its map_size corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Yokoanabo sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}. The Sarcophagus was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 in {{ill|1973|en|1973|WD=Q2477|lt=Showa 48|1=1973}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|en|Eifuku-ji|WD=Q11410732|lt=Eifuku-ji|1=Eifuku-ji}}. (Relocated and exhibited at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba, Taishi Town) |alternate_name = |affiliation = {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |location = {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|en|Taishi, Osaka|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi, Osaka|1=Taishi, Osaka}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|en|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|WD=Q1347323|lt=Minamikawachi District, Osaka|1=Minamikawachi District, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}} |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 31|coordinates = 08.7 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 38|coordinates = 25.2 |omitted = JP-27 |type = (Presumed) {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |size = |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(With house-shaped Sarcophagus-type sekkaku inside) |burial goods = Human remains, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} |satellite = |built = Mid-7th century |buried_person = |tomb_Historic Site = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = Sarcophagus (Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1) |special_notes = Mound no longer extant |map_type = Square Kofun |map_dot_label = Matsuizuka Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Matsuizuka Kofun''' (松井塚古墳) was a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|en|Taishi, Osaka|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi, Osaka|1=Taishi, Osaka}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|en|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|WD=Q1347323|lt=Minamikawachi District, Osaka|1=Minamikawachi District, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It was part of the {{ill|Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|ja|磯長谷古墳群|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. The Sarcophagus from this kofun has been Historic Site1 as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The Kofun itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijō|en|Mount Nijō|WD=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō|1=Mount Nijō}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|en|1958|WD=Q5253|lt=Showa 33|1=1958}} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s type is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang|1=Q10922645|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10922645}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|en|Mount Nijō|WD=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō|1=Mount Nijō}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko|1=Empress Suiko}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Retypes|ja|大化改新|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taika Retypes|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大化の改新|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大化の改新|1=Taika Reform|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q570140}}, as its map_size corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Yokoanabo sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}. The Sarcophagus was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 in {{ill|1973|en|1973|WD=Q2477|lt=Showa 48|1=1973}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|en|Eifuku-ji|WD=Q11410732|lt=Eifuku-ji|1=Eifuku-ji}}. The Sarcophagus from this kofun has been Historic Site1 as a tangible cultural property by Osaka Prefecture. The Kofun itself no longer exists. == Overview == Matsuizuka Kofun was constructed on the southern slope of a ridge in the Isonokami valley, located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Nijō|en|Mount Nijō|qid=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō|1=Mount Nijō}} in southeastern Osaka Prefecture{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. It was discovered in {{ill|1958|en|1958|qid=Q5253|lt=Showa 33|1=1958}} during a well-digging operation at a private residenceOn-site explanation board.. Later that year, the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education conducted an excavation survey{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The mound’s type is unknown but is presumed to have been square{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the south. The chamber construction likely used the {{ill|Tang|ja|唐尺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang|1=Q10922645|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10922645}} measurement system. Pebbles were spread across the chamber floor, and atop them was placed a house-shaped sekkaku made of tuff sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|en|Mount Nijō|qid=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō|1=Mount Nijō}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. The sekkaku measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.3 meters in width{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}, and it includes a stone door at the front{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The sekkaku had been looted before discovery, but human remains (possibly of two individuals) and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} were recovered{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代終末期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. Initially, the site drew attention as a potential {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (accompanying tomb) of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|qid=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko|1=Empress Suiko}}[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/kanko_info/rekishi_shiseki/matsuitsukakofun.html Matsuizuka Kofun Sekkaku] (Taishi Town Website).. The stone chamber’s size also remains significant for studies of burial customs under the {{ill|Taika Retypes|ja|大化改新|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taika Retypes|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大化の改新|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大化の改新|1=Taika Reform|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q570140}}, as its map_size corresponds to burials of royal status{{Harvid|Matsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The kofun is also an important example for understanding the transition of {{ill|Yokoanabo sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}. The Sarcophagus was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 in {{ill|1973|en|1973|qid=Q2477|lt=Showa 48|1=1973}} and has been relocated and displayed at Taishi Nagomi-no-Hiroba near {{ill|Eifuku-ji|en|Eifuku-ji|qid=Q11410732|lt=Eifuku-ji|1=Eifuku-ji}}.
